---
title: "Comparative Study of MCMC Sampling Strategies for the 2D Ising Model"
subtitle: "Course 9250 (Computational Statistics), Spring 2026"
author: "Abhisek Banerjee and Mengyan Jing"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    keep_tex: false
geometry: margin=1in
fontsize: 11pt
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{amsmath, amssymb}
  - \usepackage{bm}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \floatplacement{figure}{H}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  cache       = TRUE,
  cache.path  = "ising_cache/",
  fig.path    = "ising_figs/",
  fig.align   = "center",
  fig.width   = 8,
  fig.height  = 5,
  comment     = "",         # suppress ## prefix on console output
  message     = FALSE,
  warning     = FALSE,
  collapse    = FALSE
)
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(Rcpp))
```

\newpage

# Introduction

## Motivation

The two-dimensional Ising model is a foundational testbed in computational
statistics for two reasons that rarely coincide. First, it has a continuous
phase transition with a known infinite-volume critical temperature
$T_c = 2/\ln(1+\sqrt{2}) \approx 2.269$ and a closed-form solution due
to Onsager (1944) for the energy and specific heat, and Yang (1952) for
the spontaneous magnetization. Second, near $T_c$, every textbook MCMC
sampler degrades dramatically due to *critical slowing down*: the
integrated autocorrelation time $\tau$ grows as $L^z$ where $L$ is the
linear lattice size and $z \approx 2.17$ for single-spin Metropolis
(Nightingale & Bl\"ote 1996). The combination of an exact ground truth
and a known computational pathology makes the 2D Ising model a clean
benchmark for evaluating MCMC sampling strategies.

This project follows the directive of the course instructor to focus on
implementation depth over breadth: implement Metropolis and Wolff
samplers from scratch, characterize their mixing behavior near and away
from $T_c$, estimate observables across temperature, and apply the
block bootstrap as the single advanced uncertainty quantification (UQ)
component.

## The model

A 2D Ising configuration on an $L \times L$ lattice with periodic
boundary conditions is a vector of spins
$\bm{s} = (s_1, \ldots, s_N)$, $s_i \in \{-1, +1\}$, $N = L^2$. With
ferromagnetic coupling $J = 1$ and zero external field, the Hamiltonian
(Newman & Barkema 1999, Eq. 3.1) is
$$
H(\bm{s}) = -J \sum_{\langle i, j \rangle} s_i s_j,
$$
where $\langle i, j \rangle$ runs over nearest-neighbor pairs.
We work in units where Boltzmann's constant $k_B = 1$, so $\beta = 1/T$.
Quantities of interest, all per spin, are the energy
$u = \langle H \rangle / N$, the spontaneous magnetization
$|m| = \langle |\sum_i s_i| \rangle / N$, the specific heat
$c = (\langle H^2 \rangle - \langle H \rangle^2) / (N T^2)$,
and the magnetic susceptibility
$\chi = (\langle M^2 \rangle - \langle |M| \rangle^2) / (N T)$.

## Algorithms compared

**Metropolis-Hastings** with single-spin updates: at each Monte Carlo
step a random site $i$ is selected and the flip $s_i \to -s_i$ is
accepted with probability $\min(1, e^{-\Delta E / T})$. One *sweep*
consists of $N$ attempted flips. Metropolis is exact (satisfies detailed
balance) but suffers severe critical slowing down because all moves are
local while correlation length diverges at $T_c$.

**Wolff cluster algorithm** (Wolff 1989): at each step a seed site is
chosen uniformly and a same-sign cluster is grown by adding aligned
neighbors with probability $p_{\text{add}} = 1 - e^{-2\beta J}$ until
the growth halts. The entire cluster is then flipped at acceptance 1.
The cluster size adapts to the correlation length, which makes Wolff
exceptionally efficient near $T_c$ where local single-spin dynamics
fails. We refer to one cluster build-and-flip as one *cluster flip*.

## Uncertainty quantification

We use the moving (non-overlapping) block bootstrap (K\"unsch 1989) to
produce honest standard errors from autocorrelated chains. Block length
is chosen as $\lceil 2 \tau_{\text{int}} \rceil$ where
$\tau_{\text{int}}$ is the integrated autocorrelation time estimated by
Sokal's automatic windowing rule with constant $c = 5$. Different
block lengths are used for $E$-derived observables ($e$, $c$) and
$M$-derived observables ($|m|$, $\chi$), reflecting their distinct
autocorrelation structures.

## What we deliver

The proposal listed five deliverables. This document presents one
section per deliverable plus the implementation infrastructure required
to produce them. The headline results are summarized in the final
Discussion section.

\newpage

# Setup

```{r setup-constants, echo=TRUE}
J_COUPLING  <- 1
K_BOLTZMANN <- 1
T_CRITICAL  <- 2 / log(1 + sqrt(2))     # Onsager 1944, Eq. 17

# Literature dynamic critical exponents (used in Sections 8 and 9)
Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE <- 2.1665   # Nightingale & Blote 1996, PRL 76, 4548
Z_WOLFF_LITERATURE      <- 0.25     # Wolff 1989, PRL 62, 361

cat(sprintf("T_c = %.6f\n", T_CRITICAL))
```

# Lattice utilities

The lattice is stored as an $L \times L$ integer matrix of $\pm 1$
values with periodic boundary conditions implemented via modular
indexing.

```{r lattice-utils, echo=TRUE}
init_lattice <- function(L, state = c("cold", "hot", "ground")) {
  state <- match.arg(state)
  if (state %in% c("cold", "ground")) {
    matrix(1L, nrow = L, ncol = L)
  } else {
    matrix(sample(c(-1L, 1L), L * L, replace = TRUE), nrow = L, ncol = L)
  }
}

total_energy <- function(spins) {
  L <- nrow(spins); stopifnot(ncol(spins) == L)
  right <- spins[, c(2:L, 1)]
  down  <- spins[c(2:L, 1), ]
  -J_COUPLING * sum(spins * right + spins * down)
}

total_magnetization <- function(spins) sum(spins)

delta_energy <- function(spins, i, j) {
  L  <- nrow(spins)
  ip <- if (i == L)  1L else i + 1L
  im <- if (i == 1L) L  else i - 1L
  jp <- if (j == L)  1L else j + 1L
  jm <- if (j == 1L) L  else j - 1L
  neighbor_sum <- spins[ip, j] + spins[im, j] + spins[i, jp] + spins[i, jm]
  2 * J_COUPLING * spins[i, j] * neighbor_sum
}
```

\newpage

# Onsager and Yang exact ground truth

Onsager's solution gives closed-form expressions for the energy per spin
$u(T)$ (Eq.\ 116) and specific heat $c(T)$ (Eq.\ 117) involving complete
elliptic integrals. Yang's 1952 result gives the spontaneous
magnetization $|m|(T) = (1 - \sinh^{-4}(2\beta J))^{1/8}$ for $T < T_c$.
The complete elliptic integral $K(k)$ diverges as $k \to 1$, which
happens exactly at $T_c$, requiring numerical guards.

```{r ground-truth, echo=FALSE}
elliptic_K <- function(k) {
  if (abs(k) >= 1 - 1e-10) return(Inf)
  integrand <- function(theta) 1 / sqrt(1 - k^2 * sin(theta)^2)
  integrate(integrand, 0, pi / 2, rel.tol = 1e-8, subdivisions = 2000L)$value
}

elliptic_E <- function(k) {
  integrand <- function(theta) sqrt(1 - k^2 * sin(theta)^2)
  integrate(integrand, 0, pi / 2, rel.tol = 1e-8, subdivisions = 2000L)$value
}

onsager_energy <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    beta <- 1 / Ti; bj <- beta * J_COUPLING
    s2   <- sinh(2 * bj); c2 <- cosh(2 * bj)
    k1   <- 2 * s2 / c2^2
    K1   <- elliptic_K(k1)
    t2   <- tanh(2 * bj)^2
    -J_COUPLING * (c2 / s2) * (1 + (2 / pi) * (2 * t2 - 1) * K1)
  }, numeric(1))
}

onsager_specific_heat <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    beta <- 1 / Ti; bj <- beta * J_COUPLING
    s2   <- sinh(2 * bj); c2 <- cosh(2 * bj)
    k1   <- 2 * s2 / c2^2
    K1   <- elliptic_K(k1); E1 <- elliptic_E(k1)
    k1pp <- 2 * tanh(2 * bj)^2 - 1
    coth2 <- c2 / s2
    pre  <- (2 / pi) * (bj * coth2)^2
    pre * (2 * K1 - 2 * E1 - (1 - k1pp) * (pi / 2 + k1pp * K1))
  }, numeric(1))
}

onsager_specific_heat_asymptotic <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    beta <- 1 / Ti; bj <- beta * J_COUPLING
    k1   <- 2 * sinh(2 * bj) / cosh(2 * bj)^2
    K1   <- elliptic_K(k1)
    (2 / pi) * log(1 / tan(pi / 8))^2 * (K1 - 1 - pi / 4)
  }, numeric(1))
}

onsager_magnetization <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    if (Ti >= T_CRITICAL) return(0)
    beta <- 1 / Ti
    (1 - sinh(2 * beta * J_COUPLING)^(-4))^(1 / 8)
  }, numeric(1))
}

# Safe wrappers shift away from T_c by .SHIFT to avoid integrate() roundoff
.EPS_TC    <- 1e-9
.SHIFT     <- 1e-3
.SHIFT_LOG <- 1e-3

safe_onsager_energy <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    if (abs(Ti - T_CRITICAL) < .EPS_TC) onsager_energy(T_CRITICAL - .SHIFT)
    else                                 onsager_energy(Ti)
  }, numeric(1))
}

safe_onsager_magnetization <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    if (Ti >= T_CRITICAL - .EPS_TC) 0
    else                             onsager_magnetization(Ti)
  }, numeric(1))
}

safe_onsager_specific_heat <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    if (abs(Ti - T_CRITICAL) < .EPS_TC) Inf
    else                                 onsager_specific_heat(Ti)
  }, numeric(1))
}

onsager_specific_heat_proxy <- function(T) {
  vapply(T, function(Ti) {
    if (abs(Ti - T_CRITICAL) < .EPS_TC) onsager_specific_heat(T_CRITICAL - .SHIFT_LOG)
    else                                 onsager_specific_heat(Ti)
  }, numeric(1))
}

onsager_energy_curve <- function(T_grid, guard = 0.015) {
  result  <- numeric(length(T_grid))
  near_tc <- abs(T_grid - T_CRITICAL) < guard
  result[near_tc]  <- NA
  result[!near_tc] <- onsager_energy(T_grid[!near_tc])
  result
}

onsager_specific_heat_curve <- function(T_grid, guard = 0.015) {
  result  <- numeric(length(T_grid))
  near_tc <- abs(T_grid - T_CRITICAL) < guard
  result[near_tc]  <- NA
  result[!near_tc] <- sapply(T_grid[!near_tc], onsager_specific_heat)
  result
}

onsager_magnetization_curve <- function(T_grid, guard = 0.015) {
  result  <- numeric(length(T_grid))
  near_tc <- abs(T_grid - T_CRITICAL) < guard
  result[near_tc]  <- NA
  result[!near_tc] <- onsager_magnetization(T_grid[!near_tc])
  result
}

plot_onsager_ground_truth <- function() {
  T_grid <- seq(1, 4, by = 0.01)
  par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
  
  plot(T_grid, onsager_energy_curve(T_grid), type = "l", col = "darkblue", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "u(T)", main = "Energy per spin")
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  
  plot(T_grid, onsager_specific_heat_curve(T_grid), type = "l",
       col = "darkred", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "c(T)", main = "Specific heat per spin")
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  
  plot(T_grid, onsager_magnetization_curve(T_grid), type = "l",
       col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "|m|(T)", main = "Spontaneous magnetization")
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  
  plot.new()
  text(0.5, 0.5, sprintf("T_c = %.4f", T_CRITICAL), cex = 1.5)
  
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}
```

```{r ground-truth-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Onsager and Yang ground-truth curves used as reference throughout the project."}
plot_onsager_ground_truth()
```

\newpage

# Sanity checks

We verify the lattice utilities and ground-truth formulas before any
MCMC. All 14 assertions pass.

```{r sanity-checks, echo=FALSE}
run_sanity_checks <- function() {
  cat("=== Sanity checks ===\n")
  L <- 8
  s <- init_lattice(L, "cold")
  E_cold <- total_energy(s); expected <- -2 * J_COUPLING * L^2
  cat(sprintf("  [01] Cold lattice energy: got %g, expected %g -- %s\n",
              E_cold, expected, if (E_cold == expected) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  M_cold <- total_magnetization(s)
  cat(sprintf("  [02] Cold lattice magnetization: got %d, expected %d -- %s\n",
              M_cold, L^2, if (M_cold == L^2) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  dE <- delta_energy(s, 4, 4)
  cat(sprintf("  [03] dE flipping one spin in cold lattice: got %g, expected 8 -- %s\n",
              dE, if (dE == 8) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  s2 <- s; s2[4, 4] <- -s2[4, 4]; E_new <- total_energy(s2)
  cat(sprintf("  [04] Incremental vs. full energy after flip: %g vs %g -- %s\n",
              E_cold + dE, E_new,
              if (isTRUE(all.equal(E_cold + dE, E_new))) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  dE_corner <- delta_energy(s, 1, 1)
  cat(sprintf("  [05] dE at corner (PBC check): got %g, expected 8 -- %s\n",
              dE_corner, if (dE_corner == 8) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  cat(sprintf("  [06] T_c = %.6f (Onsager)\n", T_CRITICAL))
  u_Tc <- onsager_energy(T_CRITICAL - 1e-3)
  cat(sprintf("  [07] u(T_c - 1e-3): got %.6f, expected ~%.6f -- %s\n",
              u_Tc, -sqrt(2),
              if (isTRUE(all.equal(u_Tc, -sqrt(2), tolerance = 1e-2))) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  T_near <- T_CRITICAL - 0.005
  c_full <- onsager_specific_heat(T_near)
  c_asym <- onsager_specific_heat_asymptotic(T_near)
  cat(sprintf("  [08] c(T_c - 0.005): full %.4f vs asymptotic %.4f -- %s\n",
              c_full, c_asym,
              if (isTRUE(all.equal(c_full, c_asym, tolerance = 1e-2))) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  m_low <- onsager_magnetization(1.0)
  cat(sprintf("  [09] |m|(T=1): got %.6f (should be ~1) -- %s\n",
              m_low, if (m_low > 0.999) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  m_high <- onsager_magnetization(3.0)
  cat(sprintf("  [10] |m|(T=3): got %.6f (should be 0) -- %s\n",
              m_high, if (m_high == 0) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  rel_err_far <- {
    T <- T_CRITICAL - 5e-3
    abs(onsager_specific_heat(T) - onsager_specific_heat_asymptotic(T)) /
      abs(onsager_specific_heat(T))
  }
  rel_err_near <- {
    T <- T_CRITICAL - 1e-4
    abs(onsager_specific_heat(T) - onsager_specific_heat_asymptotic(T)) /
      abs(onsager_specific_heat(T))
  }
  cat(sprintf("  [11] c_asym convergence: rel.err(dT=5e-3)=%.2e > rel.err(dT=1e-4)=%.2e -- %s\n",
              rel_err_far, rel_err_near,
              if (rel_err_near < rel_err_far) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  u_hot <- onsager_energy(100.0)
  cat(sprintf("  [12] u(T=100) high-T limit: got %.4f (should be ~0) -- %s\n",
              u_hot, if (abs(u_hot) < 0.1) "PASS" else "FAIL"))
  cat(sprintf("  [13] Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE = %.4f (Nightingale & Blote 1996)\n",
              Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE))
  cat(sprintf("  [14] Z_WOLFF_LITERATURE = %.2f (Wolff 1989: ~0.25 for 2D Ising)\n",
              Z_WOLFF_LITERATURE))
  cat("=====================\n")
}

run_sanity_checks()
```

\newpage

# Metropolis sampler (R reference + Rcpp implementation)

We implement the single-spin Metropolis algorithm twice: a pure-R
reference for transparency, and an Rcpp version for speed. The Rcpp
version precomputes the five possible values of $e^{-\Delta E / T}$
($\Delta E \in \{-8, -4, 0, 4, 8\}$) into a lookup table, eliminating
the inner-loop transcendental call.

```{r metropolis-pure-R, echo=FALSE}
metropolis_step <- function(spins, T) {
  L <- nrow(spins)
  i <- sample.int(L, 1L); j <- sample.int(L, 1L)
  dE <- delta_energy(spins, i, j)
  if (dE <= 0 || runif(1) < exp(-dE / T)) {
    s_old <- spins[i, j]; spins[i, j] <- -s_old
    list(spins = spins, dE = dE, dM = -2L * s_old, accepted = TRUE)
  } else {
    list(spins = spins, dE = 0, dM = 0L, accepted = FALSE)
  }
}

metropolis_sweep <- function(spins, T) {
  N <- nrow(spins)^2
  dE_sweep <- 0; dM_sweep <- 0L; n_accepted <- 0L
  for (k in seq_len(N)) {
    step     <- metropolis_step(spins, T)
    spins    <- step$spins
    dE_sweep <- dE_sweep + step$dE
    dM_sweep <- dM_sweep + step$dM
    if (step$accepted) n_accepted <- n_accepted + 1L
  }
  list(spins = spins, dE_sweep = dE_sweep, dM_sweep = dM_sweep,
       n_accepted = n_accepted)
}

run_metropolis <- function(L, T, n_sweeps,
                           n_burnin   = n_sweeps %/% 10,
                           init_state = "hot",
                           seed       = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  spins     <- init_lattice(L, init_state)
  E_current <- total_energy(spins)
  M_current <- total_magnetization(spins)
  for (s in seq_len(n_burnin)) {
    sweep     <- metropolis_sweep(spins, T)
    spins     <- sweep$spins
    E_current <- E_current + sweep$dE_sweep
    M_current <- M_current + sweep$dM_sweep
  }
  E_series <- numeric(n_sweeps); M_series <- integer(n_sweeps)
  total_accepted <- 0L; N <- L * L
  for (s in seq_len(n_sweeps)) {
    sweep          <- metropolis_sweep(spins, T)
    spins          <- sweep$spins
    E_current      <- E_current + sweep$dE_sweep
    M_current      <- M_current + sweep$dM_sweep
    E_series[s]    <- E_current
    M_series[s]    <- M_current
    total_accepted <- total_accepted + sweep$n_accepted
  }
  list(
    series          = data.frame(sweep = seq_len(n_sweeps),
                                 E = E_series, M = M_series),
    acceptance_rate = total_accepted / (n_sweeps * N),
    final_spins     = spins,
    L = L, T = T, n_burnin = n_burnin, n_sweeps = n_sweeps
  )
}

compute_observables <- function(run) {
  N <- run$L^2; T <- run$T
  E <- run$series$E; M <- run$series$M
  list(
    e               = mean(E) / N,
    m               = mean(abs(M)) / N,
    c               = (mean(E^2) - mean(E)^2)   / (N * T^2),
    chi             = (mean(M^2) - mean(abs(M))^2) / (N * T),
    acceptance_rate = run$acceptance_rate
  )
}
```

The Rcpp implementation:

```{r metropolis-rcpp, echo=TRUE}
Rcpp::cppFunction('
List run_metropolis_cpp_inner(int L, double T, int n_sweeps, int n_burnin,
                              IntegerVector init_spins) {
  int N = L * L;
  std::vector<int> spins(N);
  for (int k = 0; k < N; k++) spins[k] = init_spins[k];

  long long E_current = 0, M_current = 0;
  for (int i = 0; i < L; i++) {
    for (int j = 0; j < L; j++) {
      int s       = spins[i * L + j];
      int s_right = spins[i * L + ((j + 1) % L)];
      int s_down  = spins[((i + 1) % L) * L + j];
      E_current  -= s * s_right + s * s_down;
      M_current  += s;
    }
  }

  // dE in {-8, -4, 0, 4, 8}: five precomputed exp(-dE/T)
  double exp_table[5];
  for (int idx = 0; idx < 5; idx++) {
    int dE = -8 + 4 * idx;
    exp_table[idx] = std::exp(-((double)dE) / T);
  }

  NumericVector E_series(n_sweeps), M_series(n_sweeps);
  long long total_accepted = 0, total_attempts = 0;
  GetRNGstate();

  int total_sweeps = n_burnin + n_sweeps;
  for (int sweep = 0; sweep < total_sweeps; sweep++) {
    for (int step = 0; step < N; step++) {
      int i = (int)(::unif_rand() * L);
      int j = (int)(::unif_rand() * L);
      if (i == L) i = L - 1;
      if (j == L) j = L - 1;

      int s_k = spins[i * L + j];
      int ip  = (i + 1) % L;
      int im  = (i + L - 1) % L;
      int jp  = (j + 1) % L;
      int jm  = (j + L - 1) % L;

      int neighbor_sum = spins[ip * L + j] + spins[im * L + j]
                       + spins[i * L + jp] + spins[i * L + jm];
      int dE = 2 * s_k * neighbor_sum;

      bool accept;
      if (dE <= 0) {
        accept = true;
      } else {
        double p = exp_table[(dE + 8) / 4];
        accept   = (::unif_rand() < p);
      }

      if (accept) {
        spins[i * L + j] = -s_k;
        E_current       += dE;
        M_current       += -2 * s_k;
        if (sweep >= n_burnin) total_accepted++;
      }
      if (sweep >= n_burnin) total_attempts++;
    }

    if (sweep >= n_burnin) {
      int idx       = sweep - n_burnin;
      E_series[idx] = (double)E_current;
      M_series[idx] = (double)M_current;
    }
  }

  PutRNGstate();
  IntegerVector final_spins(N);
  for (int k = 0; k < N; k++) final_spins[k] = spins[k];

  return List::create(
    Named("E_series")        = E_series,
    Named("M_series")        = M_series,
    Named("final_spins")     = final_spins,
    Named("acceptance_rate") = total_attempts > 0
                               ? (double)total_accepted / (double)total_attempts
                               : 0.0
  );
}
')

run_metropolis_fast <- function(L, T, n_sweeps,
                                n_burnin   = n_sweeps %/% 10,
                                init_state = "hot",
                                seed       = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  spins0    <- init_lattice(L, init_state)
  init_flat <- as.integer(spins0)
  result <- run_metropolis_cpp_inner(
    L = as.integer(L), T = as.numeric(T),
    n_sweeps = as.integer(n_sweeps), n_burnin = as.integer(n_burnin),
    init_spins = init_flat
  )
  list(
    series          = data.frame(sweep = seq_len(n_sweeps),
                                 E = result$E_series, M = result$M_series),
    acceptance_rate = result$acceptance_rate,
    final_spins     = matrix(result$final_spins, nrow = L, ncol = L),
    L = L, T = T, n_burnin = n_burnin, n_sweeps = n_sweeps
  )
}
```

## Speed benchmark: R vs Rcpp

```{r metro-bench-funcs, echo=FALSE}
benchmark_metropolis_samplers <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                          T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                          T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                                          n_sweeps = 500, seed = 1) {
  rows <- list()
  cat("=== Metropolis speed comparison ===\n")
  cat(sprintf("(n_sweeps = %d per run, seed = %d)\n\n", n_sweeps, seed))
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-11s %-7s %-9s %-9s %-9s %-7s\n",
              "L", "T_label", "T", "t_R [s]", "t_cpp [s]", "speedup", "acc"))
  cat(strrep("-", 60), "\n", sep = "")
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      t_R   <- system.time({ run_R   <- run_metropolis(     L, T, n_sweeps, seed = seed) })[["elapsed"]]
      t_cpp <- system.time({ run_cpp <- run_metropolis_fast(L, T, n_sweeps, seed = seed) })[["elapsed"]]
      speedup <- t_R / t_cpp; acc <- run_cpp$acceptance_rate
      cat(sprintf("%-3d %-11s %-7.4f %-9.3f %-9.3f %-9.1f %-7.3f\n",
                  L, label, T, t_R, t_cpp, speedup, acc))
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        L = L, T = T, T_label = label,
        t_R = t_R, t_cpp = t_cpp, speedup = speedup, acceptance = acc,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  }
  cat(strrep("-", 60), "\n", sep = "")
  bench_df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  cat(sprintf("\nMedian speedup across all (L, T): %.0fx\n", median(bench_df$speedup)))
  invisible(bench_df)
}

plot_metropolis_benchmark <- function(bench_df) {
  T_levels <- sort(unique(bench_df$T))
  T_labels <- unique(bench_df[order(bench_df$T), "T_label"])
  T_colors <- c("darkblue", "darkred", "darkgreen")[seq_along(T_levels)]
  L_levels <- sort(unique(bench_df$L))
  L_colors <- c("black", "darkorange", "purple", "darkcyan")[seq_along(L_levels)]
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  y_range <- range(c(bench_df$t_R, bench_df$t_cpp))
  plot(NA, xlim = range(L_levels), ylim = y_range, log = "xy",
       xlab = "L", ylab = "wall time [s]", main = "Wall time vs L (log-log)")
  for (k in seq_along(T_levels)) {
    sub <- bench_df[bench_df$T == T_levels[k], ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$L), ]
    lines(sub$L,  sub$t_R,   col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2, lty = 1)
    points(sub$L, sub$t_R,   col = T_colors[k], pch = 19, cex = 1.1)
    lines(sub$L,  sub$t_cpp, col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2, lty = 2)
    points(sub$L, sub$t_cpp, col = T_colors[k], pch = 17, cex = 1.1)
  }
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.7, bty = "n",
         legend = c(paste("R,", T_labels), paste("Cpp,", T_labels)),
         col = c(T_colors, T_colors),
         lty = c(rep(1, length(T_levels)), rep(2, length(T_levels))),
         pch = c(rep(19, length(T_levels)), rep(17, length(T_levels))),
         lwd = 2)
  
  sp_range <- range(bench_df$speedup)
  plot(NA, xlim = range(L_levels), ylim = sp_range, log = "x",
       xlab = "L", ylab = "speedup", main = "Speedup vs L")
  for (k in seq_along(T_levels)) {
    sub <- bench_df[bench_df$T == T_levels[k], ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$L), ]
    lines(sub$L, sub$speedup, col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2)
    points(sub$L, sub$speedup, col = T_colors[k], pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  }
  abline(h = median(bench_df$speedup), lty = 3, col = "gray50")
  legend("bottomright", cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
         legend = c(T_labels, "median"), col = c(T_colors, "gray50"),
         lty = c(rep(1, length(T_levels)), 3),
         pch = c(rep(19, length(T_levels)), NA), lwd = 2)
  
  plot(NA, xlim = range(T_levels), ylim = sp_range,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "speedup", main = "Speedup vs T")
  for (k in seq_along(L_levels)) {
    sub <- bench_df[bench_df$L == L_levels[k], ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
    lines(sub$T, sub$speedup, col = L_colors[k], lwd = 2)
    points(sub$T, sub$speedup, col = L_colors[k], pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  }
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray60")
  legend("bottomright", cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
         legend = c(paste("L =", L_levels), "T_c"),
         col = c(L_colors, "gray60"),
         lty = c(rep(1, length(L_levels)), 2),
         pch = c(rep(19, length(L_levels)), NA), lwd = 2)
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r metro-bench-run, fig.width=11, fig.height=4, fig.cap="Metropolis benchmark: Rcpp speedup of around 300x over pure R, roughly constant across $(L, T)$. Wall-time scaling exponent close to the expected $L^2$."}
bench_df <- benchmark_metropolis_samplers()
plot_metropolis_benchmark(bench_df)
```

The Rcpp version is roughly 300 times faster than the pure-R reference,
making the systematic studies in Sections 7-12 computationally feasible.

\newpage

## Validation against ground truth

For each combination of $L \in \{8, 16, 32, 64\}$ and
$T \in \{1.5, T_c, 4.0\}$ we run a Metropolis chain and compare each
estimated observable to its analytical reference. Below $T_c$ we use a
cold start (cleanest connection to the ferromagnetic ground state).
At $T_c$ we use $5000 (L/8)^2$ sweeps because critical slowing down
makes longer chains necessary at larger $L$.

```{r metro-validation-funcs, echo=FALSE}
choose_n_sweeps <- function(L, T, base_n = 5000, tol = 0.05) {
  if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < tol) as.integer(base_n * (L / 8)^2) else base_n
}
choose_init_state <- function(T) {
  if (T < T_CRITICAL - 0.1) "cold" else "hot"
}
m_finite_L_reference <- function(L, T) {
  N <- L * L
  if (T < T_CRITICAL - 0.01) {
    list(value = safe_onsager_magnetization(T),
         label = "Yang (L=inf)", regime = "below")
  } else if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 0.05) {
    list(value = NA,
         label = sprintf("~ L^(-1/8) = %.4f (scaling)", L^(-1/8)),
         regime = "critical")
  } else {
    list(value = 1 / sqrt(N),
         label = sprintf("1/sqrt(N) = %.4f", 1 / sqrt(N)),
         regime = "above")
  }
}

validate_metropolis_all_observables <- function(
    L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
    T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
    T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
    seed_base = 42) {
  rows <- list()
  cat("=== Metropolis validation against ground truths ===\n\n")
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      n_sweeps <- choose_n_sweeps(L, T)
      init     <- choose_init_state(T)
      n_burnin <- as.integer(n_sweeps / 5)
      run <- run_metropolis_fast(L, T, n_sweeps = n_sweeps, n_burnin = n_burnin,
                                 init_state = init, seed = seed_base + L)
      obs <- compute_observables(run)
      e_true     <- safe_onsager_energy(T)
      c_true     <- safe_onsager_specific_heat(T)
      c_proxy    <- onsager_specific_heat_proxy(T)
      m_ref_info <- m_finite_L_reference(L, T)
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        L = L, T = T, T_label = label,
        n_sweeps = n_sweeps, init = init, acc = obs$acceptance_rate,
        e_mcmc = obs$e, e_true = e_true, e_err = obs$e - e_true,
        c_mcmc = obs$c, c_true = c_true, c_proxy = c_proxy,
        c_err_proxy = obs$c - c_proxy,
        m_mcmc = obs$m, m_ref_val = m_ref_info$value,
        m_ref_lbl = m_ref_info$label, m_regime = m_ref_info$regime,
        chi_mcmc = obs$chi, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  }
  result <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  cat("Energy per spin (e):\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-10s %-7s %-7s %-6s %-9s %-9s %-9s\n",
              "L", "regime", "T", "n_swp", "init", "e_mcmc", "e_onsager", "err"))
  cat(strrep("-", 70), "\n", sep = "")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(result))) {
    r <- result[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-10s %-7.4f %-7d %-6s %+9.4f %+9.4f %+9.4f\n",
                r$L, r$T_label, r$T, r$n_sweeps, r$init,
                r$e_mcmc, r$e_true, r$e_err))
  }
  cat("\nMagnetic susceptibility scaling at T_c:\n")
  sub_tc_chi <- result[result$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  sub_tc_chi <- sub_tc_chi[order(sub_tc_chi$L), ]
  fit_chi <- lm(log(chi_mcmc) ~ log(L), data = sub_tc_chi)
  cat(sprintf("  chi ~ L^%.3f (literature: 1.75)\n", coef(fit_chi)[2]))
  invisible(result)
}

plot_metropolis_validation <- function(validation_df) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(validation_df$L))
  L_colors <- c("black", "darkorange", "purple", "darkcyan")[seq_along(L_values)]
  T_curve <- seq(1.0, 4.2, length.out = 400)
  u_exact <- onsager_energy_curve(T_curve)
  c_exact <- onsager_specific_heat_curve(T_curve)
  m_exact <- onsager_magnetization_curve(T_curve)
  c_cap <- 4
  c_plot <- pmin(c_exact, c_cap)
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  plot(T_curve, u_exact, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "u(T)", main = "Energy: MCMC vs Onsager",
       ylim = range(c(u_exact, validation_df$e_mcmc), finite = TRUE))
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
    sub <- validation_df[validation_df$L == L_values[k], ]
    points(sub$T, sub$e_mcmc, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = L_colors[k])
  }
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
         legend = c(paste("L =", L_values), "Onsager"),
         col = c(L_colors, "gray40"),
         pch = c(rep(19, length(L_values)), NA),
         lwd = c(rep(NA, length(L_values)), 2))
  
  plot(T_curve, c_plot, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "c(T)",
       main = sprintf("Specific heat: MCMC vs Onsager (cap %.0f)", c_cap),
       ylim = c(0, c_cap))
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
    sub <- validation_df[validation_df$L == L_values[k], ]
    points(sub$T, sub$c_mcmc, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = L_colors[k])
  }
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, bty = "n", legend = paste("L =", L_values),
         col = L_colors, pch = 19)
  
  plot(T_curve, m_exact, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
       xlab = "T", ylab = "|m|(T)",
       main = "Magnetization: MCMC vs Yang", ylim = c(0, 1.05))
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
    sub <- validation_df[validation_df$L == L_values[k], ]
    points(sub$T, sub$m_mcmc, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = L_colors[k])
  }
  legend("bottomleft", cex = 0.8, bty = "n", legend = paste("L =", L_values),
         col = L_colors, pch = 19)
  
  plot(NA, xlim = range(validation_df$T), ylim = range(validation_df$chi_mcmc),
       xlab = "T", ylab = expression(chi(T)),
       main = "Susceptibility: MCMC")
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
    sub <- validation_df[validation_df$L == L_values[k], ]
    points(sub$T, sub$chi_mcmc, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = L_colors[k])
  }
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, bty = "n", legend = paste("L =", L_values),
         col = L_colors, pch = 19)
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r metro-validation-run, fig.width=9, fig.height=7, fig.cap="Metropolis estimates against Onsager/Yang ground truth. Agreement is excellent away from $T_c$ and shows expected finite-size rounding at $T_c$."}
validation_df <- validate_metropolis_all_observables()
plot_metropolis_validation(validation_df)
```

\newpage

# Metropolis traces across $(L, T)$

For each of the 12 cells $(L, T) \in \{8, 16, 32, 64\} \times \{1.5, T_c, 4.0\}$
we plot four observables. For $e$ and $|m|$ this is the per-sweep
time series. For $c$ and $\chi$, which are variances of the chain rather
than means, we plot the *running estimate*
$c_t = (\langle E^2\rangle_{1:t} - \langle E\rangle_{1:t}^2)/(NT^2)$
and similarly for $\chi$.

```{r section7-funcs, echo=FALSE}
trace_runs_metropolis <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                  T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                  T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                                  seed_base = 42) {
  runs <- list()
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      n_sweeps <- choose_n_sweeps(L, T)
      init     <- choose_init_state(T)
      n_burnin <- as.integer(n_sweeps / 5)
      key <- sprintf("L%d_T%s", L,
                     if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "c" else sprintf("%.2f", T))
      run <- run_metropolis_fast(L = L, T = T, n_sweeps = n_sweeps,
                                 n_burnin = n_burnin, init_state = init,
                                 seed = seed_base + L)
      run$T_label <- label; run$key <- key
      runs[[key]] <- run
    }
  }
  invisible(runs)
}

thin_for_plot <- function(x, max_pts = 5000) {
  n <- length(x)
  if (n <= max_pts) return(seq_len(n))
  step <- ceiling(n / max_pts)
  seq(1L, n, by = step)
}

running_specific_heat <- function(E, N, T) {
  csum <- cumsum(E); csum2 <- cumsum(E^2); n <- seq_along(E)
  mean_E <- csum / n; mean_E2 <- csum2 / n
  pmax(mean_E2 - mean_E^2, 0) / (N * T^2)
}

running_susceptibility <- function(M, N, T) {
  csum2 <- cumsum(M^2); csumA <- cumsum(abs(M)); n <- seq_along(M)
  mean_M2 <- csum2 / n; mean_aM <- csumA / n
  pmax(mean_M2 - mean_aM^2, 0) / (N * T)
}

build_traces_one_observable <- function(runs, observable) {
  out <- list()
  for (key in names(runs)) {
    run <- runs[[key]]; N <- run$L^2; T <- run$T
    if (observable == "e") {
      y <- run$series$E / N; g <- safe_onsager_energy(T)
    } else if (observable == "m") {
      y <- abs(run$series$M) / N
      g <- if (T < T_CRITICAL - 0.01) safe_onsager_magnetization(T)
           else if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 0.05) NA
           else 1 / sqrt(N)
    } else if (observable == "c") {
      y <- running_specific_heat(run$series$E, N, T)
      g <- if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 0.05) onsager_specific_heat_proxy(T)
           else onsager_specific_heat(T)
    } else if (observable == "chi") {
      y <- running_susceptibility(run$series$M, N, T); g <- NA
    }
    out[[key]] <- list(key = key, L = run$L, T = T, T_label = run$T_label,
                       x = seq_along(y), y = y, ground = g,
                       acc_rate = run$acceptance_rate)
  }
  out
}

plot_observable_grid <- function(traces, observable,
                                 L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                 T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                 T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                                 max_pts = 5000) {
  y_all <- c()
  for (tr in traces) {
    n <- length(tr$y)
    cut <- max(2L, as.integer(0.02 * n))
    y_all <- c(y_all, tr$y[cut:n])
  }
  y_all <- y_all[is.finite(y_all)]
  y_range <- range(y_all)
  y_range <- y_range + diff(y_range) * c(-0.05, 0.05)
  
  obs_label <- switch(observable,
                      e = "e (energy per spin)",
                      m = "|m| (magnetization per spin)",
                      c = "c running estimate",
                      chi = expression(chi ~ "running estimate"))
  obs_title <- switch(observable,
                      e = "Energy trace", m = "Magnetization trace",
                      c = "Specific heat (running)",
                      chi = "Susceptibility (running)")
  obs_color <- switch(observable, e = "darkblue", m = "darkgreen",
                      c = "darkred", chi = "purple")
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(length(L_values), length(T_values)),
                 mar = c(3.2, 3.6, 2.0, 0.6), mgp = c(2.0, 0.6, 0),
                 oma = c(0, 0, 2.4, 0), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]
      key <- sprintf("L%d_T%s", L,
                     if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "c" else sprintf("%.2f", T))
      tr <- traces[[key]]
      idx <- thin_for_plot(tr$x, max_pts)
      plot(tr$x[idx], tr$y[idx], type = "l", col = obs_color, lwd = 1,
           xlab = "sweep", ylab = obs_label, ylim = y_range,
           main = sprintf("L=%d, T=%s (acc=%.2f)", L,
                          if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "T_c" else sprintf("%.2f", T),
                          tr$acc_rate),
           cex.main = 0.9)
      if (is.finite(tr$ground)) abline(h = tr$ground, lty = 2, col = "gray30", lwd = 1.2)
    }
  }
  mtext(obs_title, side = 3, outer = TRUE, line = 0.6, cex = 1.2, font = 2)
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r section7-runs}
section7_runs <- trace_runs_metropolis()
```

```{r section7-plots-e, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Metropolis energy trace across $(L, T)$. Convergence is good in all cells; chains fluctuate around the Onsager reference (dashed)."}
plot_observable_grid(build_traces_one_observable(section7_runs, "e"), "e")
```

```{r section7-plots-m, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Metropolis magnetization trace. At $T_c$ for $L=64$ the chain barely moves over 320,000 sweeps -- a clear signal of critical slowing down."}
plot_observable_grid(build_traces_one_observable(section7_runs, "m"), "m")
```

```{r section7-plots-c, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Metropolis specific heat running estimate. At $T_c$ the running estimate continues to drift across the chain length even at $L=64$."}
plot_observable_grid(build_traces_one_observable(section7_runs, "c"), "c")
```

```{r section7-plots-chi, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Metropolis susceptibility running estimate. At $T_c$ for $L=64$ the running estimate is still drifting at the end of the chain."}
plot_observable_grid(build_traces_one_observable(section7_runs, "chi"), "chi")
```

\newpage

# Metropolis autocorrelation diagnostics

We compute the integrated autocorrelation time $\tau_E$ and $\tau_M$
using Sokal's automatic windowing rule, then fit
$\tau \sim L^z$ at $T_c$ to extract the dynamic critical exponent.

```{r section8-funcs, echo=FALSE}
autocorrelation <- function(x, max_lag = NULL) {
  n <- length(x)
  if (is.null(max_lag)) max_lag <- min(n %/% 4, 1000)
  x_centered <- x - mean(x)
  v0 <- mean(x_centered^2)
  if (v0 == 0) return(c(1, rep(0, max_lag)))
  rho <- numeric(max_lag + 1); rho[1] <- 1
  for (t in seq_len(max_lag)) {
    rho[t + 1] <- mean(x_centered[1:(n - t)] * x_centered[(t + 1):n]) / v0
  }
  rho
}

integrated_tau <- function(x, c = 5, max_lag = NULL) {
  n <- length(x)
  if (is.null(max_lag)) max_lag <- n %/% 4
  rho <- autocorrelation(x, max_lag = max_lag)
  tau_partial <- 0.5 + cumsum(rho[-1])
  W_grid <- seq_along(tau_partial)
  ok <- W_grid >= c * tau_partial
  if (any(ok)) {
    W <- which(ok)[1]; closed <- TRUE
  } else {
    W <- max_lag; closed <- FALSE
  }
  list(tau = max(tau_partial[W], 0.5), window = W, rho = rho,
       closed = closed, max_lag = max_lag)
}

cell_diagnostics <- function(run) {
  N <- run$L^2
  E_per_spin <- run$series$E / N
  m_per_spin <- abs(run$series$M) / N
  diag_E <- integrated_tau(run$series$E)
  diag_M <- integrated_tau(run$series$M)
  n <- nrow(run$series)
  list(L = run$L, T = run$T, T_label = run$T_label, n_sweeps = n,
       acceptance = run$acceptance_rate,
       tau_E = diag_E$tau, tau_M = diag_M$tau,
       window_E = diag_E$window, window_M = diag_M$window,
       closed_E = diag_E$closed, closed_M = diag_M$closed,
       ESS_E = n / (2 * diag_E$tau), ESS_M = n / (2 * diag_M$tau),
       se_e = sd(E_per_spin) * sqrt(2 * diag_E$tau / n),
       se_m = sd(m_per_spin) * sqrt(2 * diag_M$tau / n),
       rho_E = diag_E$rho, rho_M = diag_M$rho)
}

build_diagnostics_table <- function(runs) {
  rows <- list()
  for (key in names(runs)) {
    d <- cell_diagnostics(runs[[key]])
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(
      L = d$L, T = d$T, T_label = d$T_label,
      n_sweeps = d$n_sweeps, acc = d$acceptance,
      tau_E = d$tau_E, tau_M = d$tau_M,
      ESS_E = d$ESS_E, ESS_M = d$ESS_M,
      se_e = d$se_e, se_m = d$se_m,
      window_E = d$window_E, window_M = d$window_M,
      closed_E = d$closed_E, closed_M = d$closed_M,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

print_diagnostics_table <- function(diag_df) {
  cat("=== Autocorrelation diagnostics across (L, T) ===\n")
  cat("Flag '!' next to tau means Sokal windowing did NOT close.\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-10s %-8s %-8s %-12s %-12s %-9s %-9s %-9s %-9s\n",
              "L", "regime", "T", "n_swp",
              "tau_E", "tau_M", "ESS_E", "ESS_M", "se_e", "se_m"))
  cat(strrep("-", 99), "\n", sep = "")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(diag_df))) {
    r <- diag_df[i, ]
    flag_E <- if (r$closed_E) " " else "!"
    flag_M <- if (r$closed_M) " " else "!"
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-10s %-8.4f %-8d %-11.2f%s %-11.2f%s %-9.0f %-9.0f %-9.5f %-9.5f\n",
                r$L, r$T_label, r$T, r$n_sweeps,
                r$tau_E, flag_E, r$tau_M, flag_M,
                r$ESS_E, r$ESS_M, r$se_e, r$se_m))
  }
  cat(strrep("-", 99), "\n", sep = "")
}

fit_dynamic_z <- function(diag_df, observable = c("E", "M")) {
  observable <- match.arg(observable)
  tau_col <- paste0("tau_", observable)
  closed_col <- paste0("closed_", observable)
  sub <- diag_df[diag_df$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  sub <- sub[sub[[closed_col]], ]
  if (nrow(sub) < 2) {
    cat(sprintf("  z (from tau_%s): not enough closed cells\n", observable))
    return(NA)
  }
  fit <- lm(log(sub[[tau_col]]) ~ log(sub$L))
  z <- coef(fit)[2]
  cat(sprintf("  z (from tau_%s): %.3f  (literature %.4f)\n",
              observable, z, Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE))
  invisible(z)
}

plot_autocorr_grid <- function(runs, observable = c("E", "M"),
                               L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                               T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                               max_lag_plot = 200) {
  observable <- match.arg(observable)
  obs_label <- switch(observable, E = "rho_E(t)", M = "rho_M(t)")
  obs_color <- switch(observable, E = "darkblue", M = "darkred")
  obs_title <- switch(observable,
                      E = "Energy autocorrelation function",
                      M = "Magnetization autocorrelation function")
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(length(L_values), length(T_values)),
                 mar = c(3.2, 3.6, 2.0, 0.6), mgp = c(2.0, 0.6, 0),
                 oma = c(0, 0, 2.4, 0), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]
      key <- sprintf("L%d_T%s", L,
                     if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "c" else sprintf("%.2f", T))
      run <- runs[[key]]
      max_lag_here <- min(max_lag_plot, nrow(run$series) %/% 4)
      x_series <- if (observable == "E") run$series$E else run$series$M
      rho <- autocorrelation(x_series, max_lag = max_lag_here)
      diag <- integrated_tau(x_series)
      flag <- if (diag$closed) "" else "!"
      plot(0:max_lag_here, rho, type = "l", col = obs_color, lwd = 1.5,
           xlab = "lag (sweeps)", ylab = obs_label, ylim = c(-0.1, 1.05),
           main = sprintf("L=%d, T=%s   tau=%.1f%s", L,
                          if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "T_c" else sprintf("%.2f", T),
                          diag$tau, flag),
           cex.main = 0.9)
      abline(h = 0, lty = 3); abline(h = 1/exp(1), lty = 3, col = "gray60")
    }
  }
  mtext(obs_title, side = 3, outer = TRUE, line = 0.6, cex = 1.2, font = 2)
  invisible(NULL)
}

plot_tau_scaling <- function(diag_df,
                             T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                             T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                             T_colors = c("darkblue", "darkred", "darkgreen")) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(diag_df$L))
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 9), xpd = FALSE,
                 no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  draw_panel <- function(y_values, y_label, main_title, tau_col, closed_col) {
    plot(NA, xlim = range(L_values),
         ylim = range(c(y_values, 0.5), finite = TRUE),
         log = "xy", xlab = "L", ylab = y_label, main = main_title)
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]
      sub <- diag_df[diag_df$T == T, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$L), ]
      pch <- ifelse(sub[[closed_col]], 19, 4)
      lines(sub$L, sub[[tau_col]], col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2)
      points(sub$L, sub[[tau_col]], col = T_colors[k], pch = pch, cex = 1.3)
    }
    sub_tc <- diag_df[diag_df$T_label == "at Tc", ]
    sub_tc <- sub_tc[order(sub_tc$L), ]
    if (nrow(sub_tc) >= 1) {
      L_ref <- range(L_values)
      y_ref <- sub_tc[[tau_col]][1] * (L_ref / sub_tc$L[1])^Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE
      lines(L_ref, y_ref, col = "gray60", lty = 3, lwd = 1.5)
    }
    par(xpd = TRUE)
    legend(x = 10^(par("usr")[2] + 0.05 * diff(par("usr")[1:2])),
           y = 10^par("usr")[4], cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
           legend = c(T_labels, sprintf("ref L^%.2f", Z_METROPOLIS_LITERATURE)),
           col = c(T_colors, "gray60"),
           lty = c(rep(1, length(T_values)), 3),
           pch = c(rep(19, length(T_values)), NA),
           lwd = c(rep(2, length(T_values)), 1.5))
    par(xpd = FALSE)
  }
  draw_panel(diag_df$tau_E, expression(tau[E] ~ "(sweeps)"),
             "Energy autocorrelation: tau_E vs L", "tau_E", "closed_E")
  draw_panel(diag_df$tau_M, expression(tau[M] ~ "(sweeps)"),
             "Magnetization autocorrelation: tau_M vs L", "tau_M", "closed_M")
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r section8-table}
section8_diag <- build_diagnostics_table(section7_runs)
print_diagnostics_table(section8_diag)
cat("\n--- Dynamic critical exponent z (Metropolis at T_c) ---\n")
fit_dynamic_z(section8_diag, "E")
fit_dynamic_z(section8_diag, "M")
```

```{r section8-rho-E, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Metropolis energy autocorrelation function across $(L, T)$."}
plot_autocorr_grid(section7_runs, "E")
```

```{r section8-rho-M, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Metropolis magnetization autocorrelation function. At $T_c$ for $L=64$, $\\rho_M$ remains above 0.6 even at lag 200."}
plot_autocorr_grid(section7_runs, "M")
```

```{r section8-tau-scaling, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Metropolis $\\tau$ vs $L$. The slope at $T_c$ matches the literature $z = 2.17$ within 4\\% for $\\tau_M$."}
plot_tau_scaling(section8_diag)
```

The fitted dynamic critical exponent for Metropolis is $z_M \approx 2.08$,
within 4\% of the literature value 2.17 from Nightingale & Bl\"ote
(1996). The visible upturn of the $\tau$ curves at $T_c$ confirms
critical slowing down as expected.

\newpage

# Wolff cluster sampler

We implement Wolff's cluster algorithm in pure R (reference) and Rcpp
(production). Sections 9.1-9.4 follow the same structure as the
Metropolis sections: implementation, benchmark, validation. Section 9.5
provides the head-to-head comparison that is the central scientific
result of this project.

```{r wolff-pure-R, echo=FALSE}
wolff_one_cluster_R <- function(spins, T) {
  L <- nrow(spins); N <- L * L
  pad <- 1 - exp(-2 / T)
  i0 <- sample.int(L, 1L); j0 <- sample.int(L, 1L)
  sigma <- spins[i0, j0]
  stack_i <- integer(N); stack_j <- integer(N); top <- 1L
  stack_i[1] <- i0; stack_j[1] <- j0
  in_cluster <- matrix(FALSE, nrow = L, ncol = L)
  in_cluster[i0, j0] <- TRUE; cluster_size <- 1L
  while (top >= 1L) {
    i <- stack_i[top]; j <- stack_j[top]; top <- top - 1L
    ip <- if (i == L) 1L else i + 1L
    im <- if (i == 1L) L else i - 1L
    jp <- if (j == L) 1L else j + 1L
    jm <- if (j == 1L) L else j - 1L
    for (nbr in list(c(ip, j), c(im, j), c(i, jp), c(i, jm))) {
      ni <- nbr[1]; nj <- nbr[2]
      if (in_cluster[ni, nj]) next
      if (spins[ni, nj] != sigma) next
      if (runif(1) < pad) {
        in_cluster[ni, nj] <- TRUE
        top <- top + 1L
        stack_i[top] <- ni; stack_j[top] <- nj
        cluster_size <- cluster_size + 1L
      }
    }
  }
  spins[in_cluster] <- -spins[in_cluster]
  list(spins = spins, cluster_size = cluster_size, sigma = sigma)
}

run_wolff <- function(L, T, n_cluster_flips,
                      n_burnin = n_cluster_flips %/% 10,
                      init_state = "hot", seed = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  spins <- init_lattice(L, init_state)
  for (f in seq_len(n_burnin)) {
    step <- wolff_one_cluster_R(spins, T); spins <- step$spins
  }
  E_series <- numeric(n_cluster_flips); M_series <- numeric(n_cluster_flips)
  cl_sizes <- integer(n_cluster_flips)
  for (f in seq_len(n_cluster_flips)) {
    step <- wolff_one_cluster_R(spins, T); spins <- step$spins
    E_series[f] <- total_energy(spins); M_series[f] <- total_magnetization(spins)
    cl_sizes[f] <- step$cluster_size
  }
  N <- L * L
  list(series = data.frame(step = seq_len(n_cluster_flips),
                           E = E_series, M = M_series, cluster_size = cl_sizes),
       mean_cluster_size = mean(cl_sizes),
       equivalent_sweeps = n_cluster_flips * mean(cl_sizes) / N,
       final_spins = spins, L = L, T = T,
       n_burnin = n_burnin, n_cluster_flips = n_cluster_flips)
}
```

The Rcpp Wolff implementation:

```{r wolff-rcpp, echo=TRUE}
Rcpp::cppFunction('
List run_wolff_cpp_inner(int L, double T, int n_cluster_flips, int n_burnin,
                         IntegerVector init_spins) {
  int N = L * L;
  std::vector<int> spins(N);
  for (int k = 0; k < N; k++) spins[k] = init_spins[k];

  double p_add = 1.0 - std::exp(-2.0 / T);

  NumericVector E_series(n_cluster_flips);
  NumericVector M_series(n_cluster_flips);
  IntegerVector cluster_sizes(n_cluster_flips);

  std::vector<int> stack;
  stack.reserve(N);
  std::vector<int> in_cluster(N, 0);

  GetRNGstate();

  int total_flips = n_burnin + n_cluster_flips;
  for (int flip = 0; flip < total_flips; flip++) {
    std::fill(in_cluster.begin(), in_cluster.end(), 0);

    int i0 = (int)(::unif_rand() * L);
    int j0 = (int)(::unif_rand() * L);
    if (i0 == L) i0 = L - 1;
    if (j0 == L) j0 = L - 1;
    int seed_idx = i0 * L + j0;
    int sigma    = spins[seed_idx];

    stack.clear();
    stack.push_back(seed_idx);
    in_cluster[seed_idx] = 1;
    int cluster_size = 1;

    while (!stack.empty()) {
      int idx = stack.back(); stack.pop_back();
      int i = idx / L;
      int j = idx % L;

      int neigh[4];
      neigh[0] = ((i + 1)     % L) * L + j;
      neigh[1] = ((i + L - 1) % L) * L + j;
      neigh[2] = i * L + ((j + 1)     % L);
      neigh[3] = i * L + ((j + L - 1) % L);

      for (int n = 0; n < 4; n++) {
        int n_idx = neigh[n];
        if (in_cluster[n_idx])       continue;
        if (spins[n_idx] != sigma)   continue;
        if (::unif_rand() < p_add) {
          in_cluster[n_idx] = 1;
          stack.push_back(n_idx);
          cluster_size++;
        }
      }
    }

    for (int k = 0; k < N; k++) {
      if (in_cluster[k]) spins[k] = -spins[k];
    }

    if (flip >= n_burnin) {
      long long E_new = 0, M_new = 0;
      for (int i = 0; i < L; i++) {
        for (int j = 0; j < L; j++) {
          int s       = spins[i * L + j];
          int s_right = spins[i * L + ((j + 1) % L)];
          int s_down  = spins[((i + 1) % L) * L + j];
          E_new -= s * s_right + s * s_down;
          M_new += s;
        }
      }
      int idx = flip - n_burnin;
      E_series[idx]      = (double)E_new;
      M_series[idx]      = (double)M_new;
      cluster_sizes[idx] = cluster_size;
    }
  }

  PutRNGstate();

  IntegerVector final_spins(N);
  for (int k = 0; k < N; k++) final_spins[k] = spins[k];

  return List::create(
    Named("E_series")      = E_series,
    Named("M_series")      = M_series,
    Named("cluster_sizes") = cluster_sizes,
    Named("final_spins")   = final_spins
  );
}
')

run_wolff_fast <- function(L, T, n_cluster_flips,
                           n_burnin = n_cluster_flips %/% 10,
                           init_state = "hot", seed = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  spins0 <- init_lattice(L, init_state)
  init_flat <- as.integer(spins0)
  result <- run_wolff_cpp_inner(
    L = as.integer(L), T = as.numeric(T),
    n_cluster_flips = as.integer(n_cluster_flips),
    n_burnin = as.integer(n_burnin), init_spins = init_flat)
  N <- L * L
  list(series = data.frame(step = seq_len(n_cluster_flips),
                           E = result$E_series, M = result$M_series,
                           cluster_size = result$cluster_sizes),
       mean_cluster_size = mean(result$cluster_sizes),
       equivalent_sweeps = n_cluster_flips * mean(result$cluster_sizes) / N,
       final_spins = matrix(result$final_spins, nrow = L, ncol = L),
       L = L, T = T, n_burnin = n_burnin, n_cluster_flips = n_cluster_flips)
}

compute_observables_wolff <- function(run) {
  N <- run$L^2; T <- run$T
  E <- run$series$E; M <- run$series$M
  list(e = mean(E) / N, m = mean(abs(M)) / N,
       c = (mean(E^2) - mean(E)^2) / (N * T^2),
       chi = (mean(M^2) - mean(abs(M))^2) / (N * T),
       mean_cluster_size = run$mean_cluster_size,
       equivalent_sweeps = run$equivalent_sweeps)
}
```

## Wolff benchmark and validation

```{r wolff-bench-funcs, echo=FALSE}
benchmark_wolff_samplers <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                     T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                     T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                                     n_cluster_flips = 500, seed = 1) {
  rows <- list()
  cat("=== Wolff speed comparison ===\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-11s %-7s %-9s %-9s %-9s %-12s\n",
              "L", "T_label", "T", "t_R [s]", "t_cpp [s]", "speedup", "mean_cluster"))
  cat(strrep("-", 66), "\n", sep = "")
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      t_R <- system.time({ run_R <- run_wolff(L, T, n_cluster_flips, seed = seed) })[["elapsed"]]
      t_cpp <- system.time({ run_cpp <- run_wolff_fast(L, T, n_cluster_flips, seed = seed) })[["elapsed"]]
      speedup <- t_R / t_cpp
      mean_cl <- run_cpp$mean_cluster_size
      cat(sprintf("%-3d %-11s %-7.4f %-9.3f %-9.3f %-9.1f %-12.1f\n",
                  L, label, T, t_R, t_cpp, speedup, mean_cl))
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        L = L, T = T, T_label = label, t_R = t_R, t_cpp = t_cpp,
        speedup = speedup, mean_cluster = mean_cl, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  }
  cat(strrep("-", 66), "\n", sep = "")
  bench_df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  cat(sprintf("\nMedian speedup: %.0fx\n", median(bench_df$speedup)))
  invisible(bench_df)
}

choose_n_cluster_flips <- function(L, T, base_n = 10000) base_n

validate_wolff_all_observables <- function(
    L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
    T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
    T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"), seed_base = 42) {
  rows <- list()
  cat("=== Wolff validation against ground truths ===\n\n")
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      n_cf <- choose_n_cluster_flips(L, T)
      run <- run_wolff_fast(L, T, n_cluster_flips = n_cf,
                            n_burnin = as.integer(n_cf / 5),
                            init_state = "hot", seed = seed_base + L)
      obs <- compute_observables_wolff(run)
      e_true <- safe_onsager_energy(T)
      m_ref_info <- m_finite_L_reference(L, T)
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        L = L, T = T, T_label = label, n_cluster_flips = n_cf,
        mean_cluster = obs$mean_cluster_size,
        equiv_sweeps = obs$equivalent_sweeps,
        e_mcmc = obs$e, e_true = e_true, e_err = obs$e - e_true,
        c_mcmc = obs$c, m_mcmc = obs$m, chi_mcmc = obs$chi,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  }
  result <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  cat("Susceptibility scaling at T_c:\n")
  sub_tc_chi <- result[result$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  sub_tc_chi <- sub_tc_chi[order(sub_tc_chi$L), ]
  fit_chi <- lm(log(chi_mcmc) ~ log(L), data = sub_tc_chi)
  cat(sprintf("  chi ~ L^%.3f (literature: 1.75)\n", coef(fit_chi)[2]))
  cat("\n|m| at T_c finite-size scaling check (expect L^-1/8):\n")
  sub_tc <- result[result$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  sub_tc <- sub_tc[order(sub_tc$L), ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub_tc))) {
    r <- sub_tc[i, ]
    pred <- sub_tc$m_mcmc[1] * (r$L / sub_tc$L[1])^(-1/8)
    cat(sprintf("  L=%2d: m = %.4f, predicted = %.4f, ratio = %.3f\n",
                r$L, r$m_mcmc, pred, r$m_mcmc / pred))
  }
  invisible(result)
}
```

```{r wolff-bench-run, fig.width=11, fig.height=4, fig.cap="Wolff R vs Rcpp benchmark."}
wolff_bench_df <- benchmark_wolff_samplers()
plot_metropolis_benchmark(wolff_bench_df)
```

```{r wolff-validation-run}
wolff_validation_df <- validate_wolff_all_observables()
```

The Wolff susceptibility scaling exponent at $T_c$ is $\chi \sim L^{1.764}$,
within 1\% of the universality-class prediction $\gamma/\nu = 7/4 = 1.75$.
The magnetization scaling check confirms $|m| \sim L^{-1/8}$ at $T_c$ to
better than 1\%.

\newpage

## Wolff traces and autocorrelation

```{r wolff-traces-funcs, echo=FALSE}
trace_runs_wolff <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                             T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                             T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                             seed_base = 42) {
  runs <- list()
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]; label <- T_labels[k]
      n_cf <- choose_n_cluster_flips(L, T)
      key <- sprintf("L%d_T%s", L,
                     if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "c" else sprintf("%.2f", T))
      run <- run_wolff_fast(L = L, T = T, n_cluster_flips = n_cf,
                            n_burnin = as.integer(n_cf / 5),
                            init_state = "hot", seed = seed_base + L)
      run$T_label <- label; run$key <- key
      runs[[key]] <- run
    }
  }
  invisible(runs)
}

build_traces_one_observable_wolff <- function(runs, observable) {
  out <- list()
  for (key in names(runs)) {
    run <- runs[[key]]; N <- run$L^2; T <- run$T
    if (observable == "e") {
      y <- run$series$E / N; g <- safe_onsager_energy(T)
    } else if (observable == "m") {
      y <- abs(run$series$M) / N
      g <- if (T < T_CRITICAL - 0.01) safe_onsager_magnetization(T)
           else if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 0.05) NA
           else 1 / sqrt(N)
    } else if (observable == "c") {
      y <- running_specific_heat(run$series$E, N, T)
      g <- if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 0.05) onsager_specific_heat_proxy(T)
           else onsager_specific_heat(T)
    } else if (observable == "chi") {
      y <- running_susceptibility(run$series$M, N, T); g <- NA
    }
    out[[key]] <- list(key = key, L = run$L, T = T, T_label = run$T_label,
                       x = seq_along(y), y = y, ground = g,
                       mean_cl = run$mean_cluster_size)
  }
  out
}

plot_observable_grid_wolff <- function(traces, observable,
                                       L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                       T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                       max_pts = 5000) {
  y_all <- c()
  for (tr in traces) {
    n <- length(tr$y)
    cut <- max(2L, as.integer(0.02 * n))
    y_all <- c(y_all, tr$y[cut:n])
  }
  y_all <- y_all[is.finite(y_all)]
  y_range <- range(y_all)
  y_range <- y_range + diff(y_range) * c(-0.05, 0.05)
  obs_label <- switch(observable,
                      e = "e (energy per spin)", m = "|m| (mag per spin)",
                      c = "c running estimate",
                      chi = expression(chi ~ "running estimate"))
  obs_title <- switch(observable,
                      e = "Wolff: Energy trace", m = "Wolff: Magnetization trace",
                      c = "Wolff: Specific heat (running)",
                      chi = "Wolff: Susceptibility (running)")
  obs_color <- switch(observable, e = "darkblue", m = "darkgreen",
                      c = "darkred", chi = "purple")
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(length(L_values), length(T_values)),
                 mar = c(3.2, 3.6, 2.0, 0.6), mgp = c(2.0, 0.6, 0),
                 oma = c(0, 0, 2.4, 0), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]
      key <- sprintf("L%d_T%s", L,
                     if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "c" else sprintf("%.2f", T))
      tr <- traces[[key]]
      idx <- thin_for_plot(tr$x, max_pts)
      plot(tr$x[idx], tr$y[idx], type = "l", col = obs_color, lwd = 1,
           xlab = "cluster flip", ylab = obs_label, ylim = y_range,
           main = sprintf("L=%d, T=%s (mean_cl=%.0f)", L,
                          if (abs(T - T_CRITICAL) < 1e-6) "T_c" else sprintf("%.2f", T),
                          tr$mean_cl), cex.main = 0.9)
      if (is.finite(tr$ground)) abline(h = tr$ground, lty = 2, col = "gray30", lwd = 1.2)
    }
  }
  mtext(obs_title, side = 3, outer = TRUE, line = 0.6, cex = 1.2, font = 2)
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r wolff-traces-runs}
section9_wolff_runs <- trace_runs_wolff()
```

```{r wolff-traces-m, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Wolff magnetization trace. Compare to Section 7: at $T_c$ for $L=64$, the chain traverses the entire $|m|$ range hundreds of times in 10,000 cluster flips, completely defeating critical slowing down."}
plot_observable_grid_wolff(build_traces_one_observable_wolff(section9_wolff_runs, "m"), "m")
```

```{r wolff-traces-e, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Wolff energy trace across $(L, T)$. At $T < T_c$ the visible wiggles reflect Wolff's near-whole-lattice cluster flips at low $T$ (each move inverts most spins); above $T_c$ the trace looks like Metropolis with white-noise structure."}
plot_observable_grid_wolff(build_traces_one_observable_wolff(section9_wolff_runs, "e"), "e")
```

```{r wolff-traces-c, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Wolff specific heat running estimate. Convergence is rapid at every $(L, T)$ -- contrast with Section 7's Metropolis figure where $L=64, T_c$ took 320k sweeps to stabilize."}
plot_observable_grid_wolff(build_traces_one_observable_wolff(section9_wolff_runs, "c"), "c")
```

```{r wolff-traces-chi, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Wolff susceptibility running estimate. Convergence is rapid at every $(L, T)$."}
plot_observable_grid_wolff(build_traces_one_observable_wolff(section9_wolff_runs, "chi"), "chi")
```

\newpage

## Wolff autocorrelation diagnostics

```{r wolff-autocorr-funcs, echo=FALSE}
cell_diagnostics_wolff <- function(run) {
  N <- run$L^2
  E_per_spin <- run$series$E / N
  m_per_spin <- abs(run$series$M) / N
  diag_E <- integrated_tau(run$series$E)
  diag_M <- integrated_tau(run$series$M)
  n <- nrow(run$series)
  sweeps_per_flip <- run$mean_cluster_size / N
  list(L = run$L, T = run$T, T_label = run$T_label,
       n_cluster_flips = n, mean_cluster = run$mean_cluster_size,
       equiv_sweeps = run$equivalent_sweeps,
       tau_E_cf = diag_E$tau, tau_M_cf = diag_M$tau,
       tau_E_sw = diag_E$tau * sweeps_per_flip,
       tau_M_sw = diag_M$tau * sweeps_per_flip,
       window_E = diag_E$window, window_M = diag_M$window,
       closed_E = diag_E$closed, closed_M = diag_M$closed,
       ESS_E = n / (2 * diag_E$tau), ESS_M = n / (2 * diag_M$tau),
       se_e = sd(E_per_spin) * sqrt(2 * diag_E$tau / n),
       se_m = sd(m_per_spin) * sqrt(2 * diag_M$tau / n))
}

build_diagnostics_table_wolff <- function(runs) {
  rows <- list()
  for (key in names(runs)) {
    d <- cell_diagnostics_wolff(runs[[key]])
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(
      L = d$L, T = d$T, T_label = d$T_label,
      n_cluster_flips = d$n_cluster_flips, mean_cluster = d$mean_cluster,
      equiv_sweeps = d$equiv_sweeps,
      tau_E_cf = d$tau_E_cf, tau_M_cf = d$tau_M_cf,
      tau_E_sw = d$tau_E_sw, tau_M_sw = d$tau_M_sw,
      ESS_E = d$ESS_E, ESS_M = d$ESS_M,
      se_e = d$se_e, se_m = d$se_m,
      window_E = d$window_E, window_M = d$window_M,
      closed_E = d$closed_E, closed_M = d$closed_M,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

print_diagnostics_table_wolff <- function(diag_df) {
  cat("=== Wolff autocorrelation diagnostics ===\n")
  cat("-- Native cluster-flip units --\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-10s %-8s %-12s %-12s %-9s %-9s\n",
              "L", "regime", "T", "tau_E_cf", "tau_M_cf", "ESS_E", "ESS_M"))
  cat(strrep("-", 78), "\n", sep = "")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(diag_df))) {
    r <- diag_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-10s %-8.4f %-12.3f %-12.3f %-9.0f %-9.0f\n",
                r$L, r$T_label, r$T, r$tau_E_cf, r$tau_M_cf,
                r$ESS_E, r$ESS_M))
  }
  cat("\n-- Equivalent-sweep units (for direct comparison with Metropolis) --\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-10s %-8s %-10s %-12s %-12s\n",
              "L", "regime", "T", "mean_cl", "tau_E_sw", "tau_M_sw"))
  cat(strrep("-", 70), "\n", sep = "")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(diag_df))) {
    r <- diag_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-10s %-8.4f %-10.1f %-12.3f %-12.3f\n",
                r$L, r$T_label, r$T, r$mean_cluster,
                r$tau_E_sw, r$tau_M_sw))
  }
}

fit_dynamic_z_wolff <- function(diag_df, observable = c("E", "M")) {
  observable <- match.arg(observable)
  tau_col <- paste0("tau_", observable, "_cf")
  closed_col <- paste0("closed_", observable)
  sub <- diag_df[diag_df$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  sub <- sub[sub[[closed_col]], ]
  if (nrow(sub) < 2) {
    cat(sprintf("  z (from tau_%s): not enough closed cells\n", observable))
    return(NA)
  }
  taus <- sub[[tau_col]]
  if (all(abs((taus - 0.5) / 0.5) < 0.02)) {
    cat(sprintf("  z (from tau_%s): all values at Sokal floor 0.5;\n", observable))
    cat(sprintf("    Wolff decorrelates this observable in <1 cluster flip at every L.\n"))
    return(NA)
  }
  fit <- lm(log(taus) ~ log(sub$L))
  cat(sprintf("  z (from tau_%s): %.3f  (literature %.2f)\n",
              observable, coef(fit)[2], Z_WOLFF_LITERATURE))
  invisible(coef(fit)[2])
}
```

```{r wolff-autocorr-table}
section9_wolff_diag <- build_diagnostics_table_wolff(section9_wolff_runs)
print_diagnostics_table_wolff(section9_wolff_diag)
cat("\n--- Dynamic critical exponent z (Wolff at T_c) ---\n")
fit_dynamic_z_wolff(section9_wolff_diag, "E")
fit_dynamic_z_wolff(section9_wolff_diag, "M")
```

The Wolff $\tau_M$ values at $T_c$ are at the Sokal floor 0.5 for every
$L$, indicating that consecutive cluster flips produce nearly
independent magnetization samples. This is the formal expression of
Wolff's success at the critical point.

\newpage

## Head-to-head comparison

This is the central scientific result. The same observables, same chain
lengths in equivalent-work units, processed by the same
autocorrelation/ESS machinery for both samplers.

```{r section9-comparison-funcs, echo=FALSE}
build_comparison_table <- function(metro_diag, wolff_diag) {
  rows <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(metro_diag))) {
    mr <- metro_diag[i, ]
    wr <- wolff_diag[wolff_diag$L == mr$L & wolff_diag$T_label == mr$T_label, ]
    if (nrow(wr) != 1) next
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      L = mr$L, T = mr$T, T_label = mr$T_label,
      metro_tau_E = mr$tau_E, metro_tau_M = mr$tau_M,
      metro_ESS_E = mr$ESS_E, metro_ESS_M = mr$ESS_M,
      wolff_tau_E_sw = wr$tau_E_sw, wolff_tau_M_sw = wr$tau_M_sw,
      wolff_ESS_E = wr$ESS_E, wolff_ESS_M = wr$ESS_M,
      ratio_tau_E = mr$tau_E / wr$tau_E_sw,
      ratio_tau_M = mr$tau_M / wr$tau_M_sw,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

print_comparison_table <- function(comp_df) {
  cat("=== Metropolis vs Wolff: tau in equivalent sweeps ===\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-10s %-8s %-12s %-12s %-12s %-12s %-10s %-10s\n",
              "L", "regime", "T", "tauE_met", "tauE_wol", "tauM_met", "tauM_wol",
              "rE", "rM"))
  cat(strrep("-", 100), "\n", sep = "")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(comp_df))) {
    r <- comp_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-10s %-8.4f %-12.3f %-12.3f %-12.3f %-12.3f %-10.1f %-10.1f\n",
                r$L, r$T_label, r$T,
                r$metro_tau_E, r$wolff_tau_E_sw,
                r$metro_tau_M, r$wolff_tau_M_sw,
                r$ratio_tau_E, r$ratio_tau_M))
  }
  cat("\n--- Headline numbers at T_c ---\n")
  sub_tc <- comp_df[comp_df$T_label == "at Tc", ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub_tc))) {
    r <- sub_tc[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("  L=%-2d : Wolff tau_E is %.1fx faster, tau_M is %.1fx faster\n",
                r$L, r$ratio_tau_E, r$ratio_tau_M))
  }
}

plot_metro_vs_wolff_tau <- function(metro_diag, wolff_diag,
                                    T_values = c(1.5, T_CRITICAL, 4.0),
                                    T_labels = c("below Tc", "at Tc", "above Tc"),
                                    T_colors = c("darkblue", "darkred", "darkgreen")) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(metro_diag$L))
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.4, 2.5, 10), xpd = FALSE,
                 no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  draw_panel <- function(observable, y_label, main_title) {
    metro_col <- paste0("tau_", observable)
    wolff_col <- paste0("tau_", observable, "_sw")
    closed_m <- paste0("closed_", observable)
    all_vals <- c(metro_diag[[metro_col]], wolff_diag[[wolff_col]])
    plot(NA, xlim = range(L_values),
         ylim = range(c(all_vals, 0.1), finite = TRUE),
         log = "xy", xlab = "L", ylab = y_label, main = main_title)
    for (k in seq_along(T_values)) {
      T <- T_values[k]
      sub <- metro_diag[metro_diag$T == T, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$L), ]
      lines(sub$L, sub[[metro_col]], col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2, lty = 1)
      points(sub$L, sub[[metro_col]], col = T_colors[k], pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
      sub <- wolff_diag[wolff_diag$T == T, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$L), ]
      lines(sub$L, sub[[wolff_col]], col = T_colors[k], lwd = 2, lty = 2)
      points(sub$L, sub[[wolff_col]], col = T_colors[k], pch = 21, cex = 1.2,
             bg = "white")
    }
    par(xpd = TRUE)
    legend(x = 10^(par("usr")[2] + 0.05 * diff(par("usr")[1:2])),
           y = 10^par("usr")[4], cex = 0.78, bty = "n",
           legend = c("Metropolis:", T_labels, "", "Wolff:", T_labels),
           col = c(NA, T_colors, NA, NA, T_colors),
           lty = c(NA, rep(1, length(T_values)), NA, NA, rep(2, length(T_values))),
           pch = c(NA, rep(19, length(T_values)), NA, NA, rep(21, length(T_values))),
           pt.bg = c(NA, rep(NA, length(T_values)), NA, NA, rep("white", length(T_values))),
           lwd = c(NA, rep(2, length(T_values)), NA, NA, rep(2, length(T_values))))
    par(xpd = FALSE)
  }
  draw_panel("E", expression(tau[E] ~ "(equiv sweeps)"),
             "tau_E vs L: Metropolis vs Wolff")
  draw_panel("M", expression(tau[M] ~ "(equiv sweeps)"),
             "tau_M vs L: Metropolis vs Wolff")
  invisible(NULL)
}

plot_rho_overlay <- function(metro_runs, wolff_runs, L = 32, max_lag_plot = 200) {
  key <- sprintf("L%d_Tc", L)
  run_m <- metro_runs[[key]]; run_w <- wolff_runs[[key]]
  rho_E_m <- autocorrelation(run_m$series$E, max_lag = max_lag_plot)
  rho_M_m <- autocorrelation(run_m$series$M, max_lag = max_lag_plot)
  rho_E_w <- autocorrelation(run_w$series$E, max_lag = max_lag_plot)
  rho_M_w <- autocorrelation(run_w$series$M, max_lag = max_lag_plot)
  sweeps_per_flip <- run_w$mean_cluster_size / (L * L)
  x_w <- (0:max_lag_plot) * sweeps_per_flip
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  plot(0:max_lag_plot, rho_E_m, type = "l", col = "darkred", lwd = 2,
       ylim = c(-0.1, 1), xlim = c(0, max_lag_plot),
       xlab = "lag (equivalent sweeps)", ylab = expression(rho[E](t)),
       main = sprintf("Energy rho at T_c, L=%d", L))
  lines(x_w, rho_E_w, col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)
  abline(h = 0, lty = 3); abline(h = 1/exp(1), lty = 3, col = "gray60")
  legend("topright", cex = 0.85, bty = "n",
         legend = c("Metropolis", "Wolff"),
         col = c("darkred", "darkblue"), lwd = 2)
  
  plot(0:max_lag_plot, rho_M_m, type = "l", col = "darkred", lwd = 2,
       ylim = c(-0.1, 1), xlim = c(0, max_lag_plot),
       xlab = "lag (equivalent sweeps)", ylab = expression(rho[M](t)),
       main = sprintf("Magnetization rho at T_c, L=%d", L))
  lines(x_w, rho_M_w, col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)
  abline(h = 0, lty = 3); abline(h = 1/exp(1), lty = 3, col = "gray60")
  legend("topright", cex = 0.85, bty = "n",
         legend = c("Metropolis", "Wolff"),
         col = c("darkred", "darkblue"), lwd = 2)
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r section9-comparison}
comparison_df <- build_comparison_table(section8_diag, section9_wolff_diag)
print_comparison_table(comparison_df)
```

```{r section9-tau-fig, fig.width=11, fig.height=5, fig.cap="$\\tau$ vs $L$ for both samplers in equivalent-sweep units. The Metropolis curves at $T_c$ rise steeply ($z \\approx 2$); the Wolff curves are nearly flat."}
plot_metro_vs_wolff_tau(section8_diag, section9_wolff_diag)
```

```{r section9-rho-fig, fig.width=10, fig.height=4.5, fig.cap="Autocorrelation function at $T_c$, $L=32$, on the same equivalent-sweep axis. Metropolis $\\rho_M$ remains near 1 throughout while Wolff $\\rho_M$ drops to zero in 1-2 equivalent sweeps."}
plot_rho_overlay(section7_runs, section9_wolff_runs, L = 32)
```

\newpage

# Full temperature sweep

A 34-temperature grid from $T = 1$ to $T = 4$, dense around $T_c$, with
Wolff at every $L \in \{8, 16, 32, 64\}$. 5000 cluster flips per cell,
136 runs total, complete in approximately 20 seconds.

```{r section10-funcs, echo=FALSE}
make_T_grid_sweep <- function() {
  sort(unique(c(seq(1.0, 1.8, by = 0.2),
                seq(1.9, 2.15, by = 0.05),
                seq(2.20, 2.34, by = 0.02),
                seq(2.40, 2.80, by = 0.05),
                seq(2.9, 4.0, by = 0.2))))
}

run_T_sweep_at_L <- function(L, T_grid, n_cluster_flips = 5000, seed_base = 42) {
  rows <- list()
  for (T in T_grid) {
    run <- run_wolff_fast(L = L, T = T,
                          n_cluster_flips = n_cluster_flips,
                          n_burnin = as.integer(n_cluster_flips / 5),
                          init_state = "hot",
                          seed = as.integer(seed_base + round(T * 1000) + L))
    obs <- compute_observables_wolff(run)
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      L = L, T = T, e = obs$e, m = obs$m, c = obs$c, chi = obs$chi,
      mean_cluster = obs$mean_cluster_size,
      equiv_sweeps = obs$equivalent_sweeps,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

run_full_T_sweep <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                             T_grid = make_T_grid_sweep(),
                             n_cluster_flips = 5000, seed_base = 42) {
  all_results <- list()
  for (L in L_values) {
    res <- run_T_sweep_at_L(L, T_grid, n_cluster_flips, seed_base)
    all_results[[as.character(L)]] <- res
  }
  do.call(rbind, all_results)
}

plot_T_sweep <- function(sweep_df) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(sweep_df$L))
  L_colors <- c("black", "darkorange", "purple", "darkcyan")[seq_along(L_values)]
  T_curve <- seq(min(sweep_df$T), max(sweep_df$T), length.out = 400)
  u_exact <- onsager_energy_curve(T_curve)
  c_exact <- onsager_specific_heat_curve(T_curve)
  m_exact <- onsager_magnetization_curve(T_curve)
  c_cap <- 4; c_plot <- pmin(c_exact, c_cap)
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  for (panel in list(
    list(y_exact = u_exact, col = "e", title = "Energy", ylim = NULL, ylab = "u(T)"),
    list(y_exact = c_plot, col = "c", title = sprintf("Specific heat (cap %d)", c_cap),
         ylim = c(0, c_cap), ylab = "c(T)"),
    list(y_exact = m_exact, col = "m", title = "Magnetization",
         ylim = c(0, 1.05), ylab = "|m|(T)"),
    list(y_exact = NULL, col = "chi", title = "Susceptibility",
         ylim = c(0, max(sweep_df$chi) * 1.05), ylab = expression(chi(T))))) {
    if (!is.null(panel$y_exact)) {
      plot(T_curve, panel$y_exact, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
           xlab = "T", ylab = panel$ylab, main = panel$title,
           ylim = if (is.null(panel$ylim))
                    range(c(panel$y_exact, sweep_df[[panel$col]]), finite = TRUE)
                  else panel$ylim)
    } else {
      plot(NA, xlim = range(T_curve), ylim = panel$ylim,
           xlab = "T", ylab = panel$ylab, main = panel$title)
    }
    abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
    for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
      sub <- sweep_df[sweep_df$L == L_values[k], ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
      points(sub$T, sub[[panel$col]], pch = 19, cex = 0.7, col = L_colors[k])
      lines(sub$T, sub[[panel$col]], col = L_colors[k], lwd = 1)
    }
    legend("topleft", cex = 0.7, bty = "n",
           legend = paste("L =", L_values), col = L_colors, pch = 19)
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}

analyze_chi_peak <- function(sweep_df) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(sweep_df$L))
  cat("=== Susceptibility peak analysis ===\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-3s %-12s %-12s %-10s\n",
              "L", "T_peak", "chi_peak", "T_peak - T_c"))
  cat(strrep("-", 45), "\n", sep = "")
  peak_rows <- list()
  for (L in L_values) {
    sub <- sweep_df[sweep_df$L == L, ]
    i_peak <- which.max(sub$chi)
    cat(sprintf("%-3d %-12.4f %-12.4f %+10.4f\n",
                L, sub$T[i_peak], sub$chi[i_peak], sub$T[i_peak] - T_CRITICAL))
    peak_rows[[length(peak_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      L = L, T_peak = sub$T[i_peak], chi_peak = sub$chi[i_peak])
  }
  peak_df <- do.call(rbind, peak_rows)
  fit_peak <- lm(log(chi_peak) ~ log(L), data = peak_df)
  cat(sprintf("\nchi_peak ~ L^%.3f  (literature: 1.75)\n", coef(fit_peak)[2]))
  invisible(peak_df)
}
```

```{r section10-run, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Full temperature sweep with Wolff. All four observables show clean agreement with Onsager/Yang ground truth, with the expected finite-size rounding of the transition at $T_c$."}
sweep_df <- run_full_T_sweep()
plot_T_sweep(sweep_df)
```

```{r section10-peak}
chi_peak_df <- analyze_chi_peak(sweep_df)
```

The susceptibility peak height scales as $\chi_{\text{peak}} \sim L^{1.78}$,
within 2\% of the universality-class prediction $\gamma/\nu = 1.75$.

\newpage

# Block bootstrap uncertainty quantification

Block bootstrap with block length $\lceil 2 \tau_{\text{int}} \rceil$
applied to all four observables at every $(L, T)$ cell, using Wolff
output. The block lengths use $\tau_E$ for $e, c$ and $\tau_M$ for
$|m|, \chi$.

```{r section11-funcs, echo=FALSE}
block_bootstrap <- function(x, stat_fn, block_length = NULL,
                            n_boot = 200, seed = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  n <- length(x)
  if (is.null(block_length)) {
    tau <- integrated_tau(x)$tau
    block_length <- max(1, ceiling(2 * tau))
  }
  block_length <- min(block_length, n %/% 4)
  block_length <- max(block_length, 1L)
  n_blocks_full <- n %/% block_length
  blocks <- matrix(x[seq_len(n_blocks_full * block_length)], nrow = block_length)
  estimate <- stat_fn(x)
  boot_values <- numeric(n_boot)
  for (b in seq_len(n_boot)) {
    chosen <- sample.int(n_blocks_full, n_blocks_full, replace = TRUE)
    boot_values[b] <- stat_fn(as.vector(blocks[, chosen]))
  }
  list(estimate = estimate, se = sd(boot_values),
       block_length = block_length, n_blocks = n_blocks_full,
       boot_values = boot_values)
}

bootstrap_all_observables <- function(run, n_boot = 200, seed = NULL) {
  N <- run$L^2; T <- run$T
  E <- run$series$E; M <- run$series$M
  tau_E <- integrated_tau(E)$tau; tau_M <- integrated_tau(M)$tau
  bl_E <- max(1, ceiling(2 * tau_E)); bl_M <- max(1, ceiling(2 * tau_M))
  s <- if (!is.null(seed)) seed else NULL
  bs_e   <- block_bootstrap(E, function(x) mean(x) / N,
                            block_length = bl_E, n_boot = n_boot,
                            seed = if (!is.null(s)) s + 1 else NULL)
  bs_c   <- block_bootstrap(E, function(x) (mean(x^2) - mean(x)^2) / (N * T^2),
                            block_length = bl_E, n_boot = n_boot,
                            seed = if (!is.null(s)) s + 2 else NULL)
