  bs_m   <- block_bootstrap(M, function(x) mean(abs(x)) / N,
                            block_length = bl_M, n_boot = n_boot,
                            seed = if (!is.null(s)) s + 3 else NULL)
  bs_chi <- block_bootstrap(M, function(x) (mean(x^2) - mean(abs(x))^2) / (N * T),
                            block_length = bl_M, n_boot = n_boot,
                            seed = if (!is.null(s)) s + 4 else NULL)
  list(e = bs_e$estimate, e_se = bs_e$se,
       c = bs_c$estimate, c_se = bs_c$se,
       m = bs_m$estimate, m_se = bs_m$se,
       chi = bs_chi$estimate, chi_se = bs_chi$se,
       tau_E = tau_E, tau_M = tau_M, bl_E = bl_E, bl_M = bl_M,
       boot_e = bs_e$boot_values, boot_c = bs_c$boot_values,
       boot_m = bs_m$boot_values, boot_chi = bs_chi$boot_values)
}

run_T_sweep_with_bootstrap <- function(L_values = c(8, 16, 32, 64),
                                       T_grid = make_T_grid_sweep(),
                                       n_cluster_flips = 5000,
                                       n_boot = 200, seed_base = 9999) {
  rows <- list()
  for (L in L_values) {
    for (T in T_grid) {
      run <- run_wolff_fast(L = L, T = T,
                            n_cluster_flips = n_cluster_flips,
                            n_burnin = as.integer(n_cluster_flips / 5),
                            init_state = "hot",
                            seed = as.integer(seed_base + round(T * 1000) + L))
      bs <- bootstrap_all_observables(run, n_boot = n_boot,
                                      seed = as.integer(seed_base + L + round(T * 100)))
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        L = L, T = T,
        e = bs$e, e_se = bs$e_se, c = bs$c, c_se = bs$c_se,
        m = bs$m, m_se = bs$m_se, chi = bs$chi, chi_se = bs$chi_se,
        tau_E = bs$tau_E, tau_M = bs$tau_M,
        bl_E = bs$bl_E, bl_M = bs$bl_M,
        mean_cluster = run$mean_cluster_size, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

plot_T_sweep_with_errorbars_zoom <- function(sweep_err_df,
                                             T_zoom_min = 2.00,
                                             T_zoom_max = 2.50) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(sweep_err_df$L))
  L_colors <- c("black", "darkorange", "purple", "darkcyan")[seq_along(L_values)]
  T_full <- seq(min(sweep_err_df$T), max(sweep_err_df$T), length.out = 400)
  T_zoom <- seq(T_zoom_min, T_zoom_max, length.out = 200)
  u_full <- onsager_energy_curve(T_full); u_zoom <- onsager_energy_curve(T_zoom)
  c_full <- onsager_specific_heat_curve(T_full); c_zoom <- onsager_specific_heat_curve(T_zoom)
  m_full <- onsager_magnetization_curve(T_full); m_zoom <- onsager_magnetization_curve(T_zoom)
  c_cap <- 4
  c_full_plot <- pmin(c_full, c_cap); c_zoom_plot <- pmin(c_zoom, c_cap)
  zoom_df <- sweep_err_df[sweep_err_df$T >= T_zoom_min & sweep_err_df$T <= T_zoom_max, ]
  
  draw_eb <- function(x, y, se, color, cex_pt = 0.9) {
    segments(x, y - se, x, y + se, col = color, lwd = 1.5)
    points(x, y, pch = 19, cex = cex_pt, col = color)
  }
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(4, 2), mar = c(3.6, 4.2, 2.0, 0.8),
                 mgp = c(2.2, 0.7, 0), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  
  panels <- list(
    list(name = "e", full_y = u_full, zoom_y = u_zoom,
         ylab = "u(T)", title = "Energy"),
    list(name = "c", full_y = c_full_plot, zoom_y = c_zoom_plot,
         ylab = "c(T)", title = "Specific heat"),
    list(name = "m", full_y = m_full, zoom_y = m_zoom,
         ylab = "|m|(T)", title = "Magnetization"),
    list(name = "chi", full_y = NULL, zoom_y = NULL,
         ylab = "chi(T)", title = "Susceptibility"))
  
  for (p in panels) {
    se_col <- paste0(p$name, "_se")
    if (!is.null(p$full_y)) {
      ylim <- range(c(p$full_y,
                      sweep_err_df[[p$name]] - sweep_err_df[[se_col]],
                      sweep_err_df[[p$name]] + sweep_err_df[[se_col]]),
                    finite = TRUE)
      plot(T_full, p$full_y, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
           xlab = "T", ylab = p$ylab,
           main = sprintf("%s: full range", p$title), ylim = ylim)
    } else {
      ylim <- c(0, max(sweep_err_df$chi + sweep_err_df$chi_se) * 1.05)
      plot(NA, xlim = range(T_full), ylim = ylim,
           xlab = "T", ylab = p$ylab,
           main = sprintf("%s: full range", p$title))
    }
    abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
    rect(T_zoom_min, par("usr")[3], T_zoom_max, par("usr")[4],
         col = rgb(1, 1, 0, 0.12), border = NA)
    for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
      sub <- sweep_err_df[sweep_err_df$L == L_values[k], ]
      sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
      draw_eb(sub$T, sub[[p$name]], sub[[se_col]], L_colors[k], cex_pt = 0.6)
    }
    
    # Zoom panel
    if (!is.null(p$zoom_y)) {
      yzoom <- c(zoom_df[[p$name]] - zoom_df[[se_col]],
                 zoom_df[[p$name]] + zoom_df[[se_col]],
                 p$zoom_y[!is.na(p$zoom_y)])
      plot(T_zoom, p$zoom_y, type = "l", col = "gray40", lwd = 2,
           xlab = "T", ylab = p$ylab,
           main = sprintf("%s: zoom [%.2f, %.2f]", p$title, T_zoom_min, T_zoom_max),
           xlim = c(T_zoom_min, T_zoom_max), ylim = range(yzoom))
    } else {
      yzoom <- c(zoom_df$chi - zoom_df$chi_se, zoom_df$chi + zoom_df$chi_se)
      plot(NA, xlim = c(T_zoom_min, T_zoom_max), ylim = c(0, max(yzoom) * 1.05),
           xlab = "T", ylab = p$ylab,
           main = sprintf("%s: zoom [%.2f, %.2f]", p$title, T_zoom_min, T_zoom_max))
    }
    abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
    for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
      sub <- zoom_df[zoom_df$L == L_values[k], ]
      sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
      draw_eb(sub$T, sub[[p$name]], sub[[se_col]], L_colors[k])
    }
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}

plot_SE_vs_T <- function(sweep_err_df) {
  L_values <- sort(unique(sweep_err_df$L))
  L_colors <- c("black", "darkorange", "purple", "darkcyan")[seq_along(L_values)]
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  for (obs_info in list(
    list(col = "e_se",   label = "SE(u)",   title = "Energy SE vs T"),
    list(col = "c_se",   label = "SE(c)",   title = "Specific heat SE vs T"),
    list(col = "m_se",   label = "SE(|m|)", title = "Magnetization SE vs T"),
    list(col = "chi_se", label = "SE(chi)", title = "Susceptibility SE vs T"))) {
    plot(NA, xlim = range(sweep_err_df$T),
         ylim = c(0, max(sweep_err_df[[obs_info$col]])),
         xlab = "T", ylab = obs_info$label, main = obs_info$title)
    abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
    for (k in seq_along(L_values)) {
      sub <- sweep_err_df[sweep_err_df$L == L_values[k], ]
      sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
      points(sub$T, sub[[obs_info$col]], pch = 19, cex = 0.7, col = L_colors[k])
      lines(sub$T, sub[[obs_info$col]], col = L_colors[k], lwd = 1)
    }
    legend("topleft", cex = 0.7, bty = "n",
           legend = paste("L =", L_values),
           col = L_colors, pch = 19, lwd = 1)
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}

sanity_check_bootstrap_vs_naive <- function(L = 32,
                                            T_grid = make_T_grid_sweep(),
                                            n_cluster_flips = 5000,
                                            n_boot = 200,
                                            seed_base = 7777) {
  cat(sprintf("Sanity: bootstrap SE vs naive SE for energy, L=%d\n\n", L))
  rows <- list()
  for (T in T_grid) {
    run <- run_wolff_fast(L = L, T = T,
                          n_cluster_flips = n_cluster_flips,
                          n_burnin = as.integer(n_cluster_flips / 5),
                          init_state = "hot",
                          seed = as.integer(seed_base + round(T * 1000)))
    N <- L^2
    e_series <- run$series$E / N
    naive_se <- sd(e_series) / sqrt(length(e_series))
    tau_E <- integrated_tau(run$series$E)$tau
    bl <- max(1, ceiling(2 * tau_E))
    bs <- block_bootstrap(run$series$E, function(Es) mean(Es) / N,
                          block_length = bl, n_boot = n_boot)
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      T = T, naive_se = naive_se, boot_se = bs$se, tau_E = tau_E,
      ratio_observed = bs$se / naive_se,
      ratio_expected = sqrt(2 * tau_E))
  }
  df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 1))
  plot(df$T, df$boot_se, type = "b", col = "darkblue", pch = 19, cex = 0.8,
       ylim = range(c(df$boot_se, df$naive_se)),
       xlab = "T", ylab = "SE(<e>)",
       main = sprintf("Bootstrap vs naive SE (L = %d)", L))
  lines(df$T, df$naive_se, type = "b", col = "darkred", pch = 17, cex = 0.8)
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.85, bty = "n",
         legend = c("Block bootstrap SE", "Naive iid SE"),
         col = c("darkblue", "darkred"), pch = c(19, 17), lty = 1)
  plot(df$T, df$ratio_observed, type = "b", col = "darkgreen",
       pch = 19, cex = 0.8,
       ylim = range(c(df$ratio_observed, df$ratio_expected), finite = TRUE),
       xlab = "T", ylab = "SE ratio (boot / naive)",
       main = sprintf("SE ratio vs sqrt(2 tau) (L = %d)", L))
  lines(df$T, df$ratio_expected, type = "b", col = "black",
        pch = 17, cex = 0.8, lty = 2)
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "gray")
  abline(h = 1, lty = 3, col = "gray50")
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.85, bty = "n",
         legend = c("observed ratio", "sqrt(2 tau_E) (theory)"),
         col = c("darkgreen", "black"), pch = c(19, 17), lty = c(1, 2))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
  cat(sprintf("Median ratio (boot/naive): %.2f\n",
              median(df$ratio_observed, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("Max ratio:  %.2f  (at T=%.3f)\n",
              max(df$ratio_observed),
              df$T[which.max(df$ratio_observed)]))
  invisible(df)
}

bootstrap_head_to_head_Tc <- function(L = 32, T = T_CRITICAL,
                                      n_samples = 10000, n_boot = 500,
                                      seed_m = 1111, seed_w = 2222) {
  cat(sprintf("Head-to-head bootstrap at L=%d, T=%.4f (n=%d, n_boot=%d)\n\n",
              L, T, n_samples, n_boot))
  run_m <- run_metropolis_fast(L = L, T = T, n_sweeps = n_samples,
                                n_burnin = as.integer(n_samples / 5),
                                init_state = "hot", seed = seed_m)
  run_w <- run_wolff_fast(L = L, T = T, n_cluster_flips = n_samples,
                           n_burnin = as.integer(n_samples / 5),
                           init_state = "hot", seed = seed_w)
  bs_m <- bootstrap_all_observables(run_m, n_boot = n_boot, seed = seed_m)
  bs_w <- bootstrap_all_observables(run_w, n_boot = n_boot, seed = seed_w)
  
  cat("--- Bootstrap diagnostics ---\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-12s %-12s %-12s %-12s %-12s\n",
              "Observable", "Metro est", "Metro SE", "Wolff est", "Wolff SE"))
  cat(strrep("-", 65), "\n", sep = "")
  for (obs in list(c("e", "e_se"), c("c", "c_se"), c("m", "m_se"), c("chi", "chi_se"))) {
    cat(sprintf("%-12s %+12.5f %-12.5f %+12.5f %-12.5f\n",
                obs[1], bs_m[[obs[1]]], bs_m[[obs[2]]],
                bs_w[[obs[1]]], bs_w[[obs[2]]]))
  }
  cat("\n--- SE ratio (Metropolis / Wolff) ---\n")
  for (obs in c("e_se", "c_se", "m_se", "chi_se")) {
    cat(sprintf("  %-6s : %.2fx wider for Metropolis\n",
                sub("_se", "", obs), bs_m[[obs]] / bs_w[[obs]]))
  }
  cat(sprintf("\nBlock counts:\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  Metropolis: bl_E=%d (%d blocks), bl_M=%d (%d blocks)\n",
              bs_m$bl_E, n_samples %/% bs_m$bl_E,
              bs_m$bl_M, n_samples %/% bs_m$bl_M))
  cat(sprintf("  Wolff:      bl_E=%d (%d blocks), bl_M=%d (%d blocks)\n",
              bs_w$bl_E, n_samples %/% bs_w$bl_E,
              bs_w$bl_M, n_samples %/% bs_w$bl_M))
  
  draw_overlay <- function(m, w, label, title) {
    all_x <- c(m, w); breaks <- seq(min(all_x), max(all_x), length.out = 40)
    hist(m, breaks = breaks, col = rgb(0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 0.5), border = NA,
         freq = FALSE, xlim = range(all_x), xlab = label, main = title)
    hist(w, breaks = breaks, col = rgb(0.2, 0.2, 0.8, 0.5), border = NA,
         freq = FALSE, add = TRUE)
    abline(v = mean(m), col = "darkred", lwd = 2)
    abline(v = mean(w), col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)
    legend("topright", cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
           legend = c(sprintf("Metro (SE=%.4f)", sd(m)),
                      sprintf("Wolff (SE=%.4f)", sd(w))),
           fill = c(rgb(0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 0.5), rgb(0.2, 0.2, 0.8, 0.5)))
  }
  
  old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2.5, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  draw_overlay(bs_m$boot_e, bs_w$boot_e, "e", "Bootstrap dist: e")
  draw_overlay(bs_m$boot_c, bs_w$boot_c, "c", "Bootstrap dist: c")
  draw_overlay(bs_m$boot_m, bs_w$boot_m, "|m|", "Bootstrap dist: |m|")
  draw_overlay(bs_m$boot_chi, bs_w$boot_chi, expression(chi),
               "Bootstrap dist: chi")
  invisible(list(metropolis = bs_m, wolff = bs_w))
}
```

```{r section11-sweep}
sweep_err_df <- run_T_sweep_with_bootstrap()
```

```{r section11-zoom-fig, fig.width=10, fig.height=11, fig.cap="Phase transition observables with block-bootstrap error bars. Left column: full $T$ range. Right column: zoom into $T \\in [2.0, 2.5]$ to make error bars visible."}
plot_T_sweep_with_errorbars_zoom(sweep_err_df)
```

```{r section11-se-vs-T, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Standard error of each observable as a function of $T$, all $L$ overlaid. Every panel peaks at $T_c$, reflecting that variance-based observables have largest sampling uncertainty exactly where the physics is most interesting."}
plot_SE_vs_T(sweep_err_df)
```

```{r section11-sanity, fig.width=10, fig.height=4.5, fig.cap="Sanity check at $L=32$: block-bootstrap SE compared to the naive iid SE for the energy. Left panel shows both estimators; right panel shows their ratio compared to the theoretical $\\sqrt{2\\tau_E}$. The observed ratio tracks theory closely, confirming the bootstrap is producing statistically honest standard errors."}
naive_vs_boot_df <- sanity_check_bootstrap_vs_naive(L = 32)
```

```{r section11-h2h, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Head-to-head bootstrap distributions at $L=32$, $T_c$. Wolff (blue) yields dramatically tighter SEs for $|m|$ and $\\chi$ than Metropolis (red), reflecting the difference in effective sample count from the two samplers' very different $\\tau_M$ values."}
h2h_result <- bootstrap_head_to_head_Tc()
```

\newpage

# T_c estimation with bootstrap CI

Final deliverable: estimate the critical temperature from the
finite-size scaling of the susceptibility peak,
$T_{\text{peak}}(L) = T_c + a/L + O(1/L^2)$, with a bootstrap CI.

The peak location $T_{\text{peak}}$ is found by fitting a parabola to
the 5 points around the argmax of $\chi(T)$ for each $L$, and the
parametric bootstrap redraws $\chi$ values from the SE estimates of
Section 11 to propagate uncertainty.

```{r section12-funcs, echo=FALSE}
peak_T_parabolic <- function(T_grid, chi_values, n_side = 2) {
  if (any(!is.finite(chi_values))) return(NA_real_)
  i_max <- which.max(chi_values)
  if (i_max <= n_side || i_max > length(chi_values) - n_side) {
    return(T_grid[i_max])
  }
  idx <- (i_max - n_side):(i_max + n_side)
  T_sub <- T_grid[idx]; c_sub <- chi_values[idx]
  fit <- tryCatch(lm(c_sub ~ T_sub + I(T_sub^2)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(T_grid[i_max])
  co <- coef(fit)
  if (is.na(co[3]) || co[3] >= 0) return(T_grid[i_max])
  T_peak <- -co[2] / (2 * co[3])
  if (T_peak < min(T_sub) || T_peak > max(T_sub)) return(T_grid[i_max])
  as.numeric(T_peak)
}

fit_Tc_from_peaks <- function(L_values, T_peaks) {
  ok <- is.finite(T_peaks)
  if (sum(ok) < 2) return(list(T_c = NA, slope = NA, fit = NULL))
  df <- data.frame(inv_L = 1 / L_values[ok], T_peak = T_peaks[ok])
  fit <- lm(T_peak ~ inv_L, data = df)
  list(T_c = as.numeric(coef(fit)[1]), slope = as.numeric(coef(fit)[2]),
       fit = fit)
}

bootstrap_Tc <- function(sweep_err_df,
                         L_values = sort(unique(sweep_err_df$L)),
                         n_boot = 1000, use_L_min = NULL, seed = 4242) {
  if (!is.null(use_L_min)) L_values <- L_values[L_values >= use_L_min]
  set.seed(seed)
  per_L <- lapply(L_values, function(L) {
    sub <- sweep_err_df[sweep_err_df$L == L, ]
    sub <- sub[order(sub$T), ]
    list(L = L, T = sub$T, chi = sub$chi, chi_se = sub$chi_se)
  })
  T_peaks_pt <- vapply(per_L, function(pl) peak_T_parabolic(pl$T, pl$chi),
                       numeric(1))
  pt_fit <- fit_Tc_from_peaks(L_values, T_peaks_pt)
  T_c_boot <- numeric(n_boot)
  T_peaks_boot <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_boot, ncol = length(L_values))
  for (b in seq_len(n_boot)) {
    T_peaks_b <- vapply(per_L, function(pl) {
      chi_draw <- pmax(rnorm(length(pl$chi), pl$chi, pl$chi_se), 0)
      peak_T_parabolic(pl$T, chi_draw)
    }, numeric(1))
    T_peaks_boot[b, ] <- T_peaks_b
    T_c_boot[b] <- fit_Tc_from_peaks(L_values, T_peaks_b)$T_c
  }
  ci <- quantile(T_c_boot, c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
  list(L_values = L_values, T_peaks = T_peaks_pt,
       T_c_point = pt_fit$T_c, slope_point = pt_fit$slope,
       T_c_boot = T_c_boot, ci_95 = ci, se = sd(T_c_boot, na.rm = TRUE),
       T_peaks_boot = T_peaks_boot, fit = pt_fit$fit)
}

print_Tc_result <- function(res, label = "") {
  if (nchar(label) > 0) cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", label))
  cat(sprintf("  Lattice sizes:        L = %s\n",
              paste(res$L_values, collapse = ", ")))
  cat(sprintf("  T_peak per L:         %s\n",
              paste(sprintf("%.4f", res$T_peaks), collapse = ", ")))
  cat(sprintf("  Slope (1/L coeff):    %.4f\n", res$slope_point))
  cat(sprintf("  T_c point estimate:   %.5f\n", res$T_c_point))
  cat(sprintf("  T_c bootstrap SE:     %.5f\n", res$se))
  cat(sprintf("  95%% CI:               [%.5f, %.5f]\n",
              res$ci_95[1], res$ci_95[2]))
  cat(sprintf("  Exact T_c (Onsager):  %.5f\n", T_CRITICAL))
  err <- res$T_c_point - T_CRITICAL
  cat(sprintf("  Point - exact:        %+.5f  (%.2f SEs away)\n",
              err, err / res$se))
  cat(sprintf("  Exact in 95%% CI?      %s\n",
              if (T_CRITICAL >= res$ci_95[1] && T_CRITICAL <= res$ci_95[2]) "YES" else "NO"))
  cat("\n")
}

plot_Tc_extrapolation <- function(res_full, res_trim = NULL) {
  L_values <- res_full$L_values; inv_L <- 1 / L_values
  old_par <- par(mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  x_plot <- c(0, max(inv_L) * 1.05)
  y_range <- range(c(res_full$T_peaks, res_full$T_c_point, T_CRITICAL,
                     res_full$ci_95), na.rm = TRUE)
  y_range <- y_range + diff(y_range) * c(-0.05, 0.05)
  
  plot(inv_L, res_full$T_peaks, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = "darkblue",
       xlim = x_plot, ylim = y_range,
       xlab = "1 / L", ylab = expression(T[peak]),
       main = "Finite-size scaling: T_peak(L) = T_c + a/L")
  if (!is.null(res_full$T_peaks_boot)) {
    for (b in seq_len(min(100, nrow(res_full$T_peaks_boot)))) {
      T_peaks_b <- res_full$T_peaks_boot[b, ]
      if (any(!is.finite(T_peaks_b))) next
      fb <- tryCatch(lm(T_peaks_b ~ inv_L), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(fb)) next
      abline(fb, col = rgb(0.2, 0.2, 0.8, 0.05), lwd = 1)
    }
  }
  abline(res_full$fit, col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)
  points(inv_L, res_full$T_peaks, pch = 19, cex = 1.3, col = "darkblue")
  abline(h = T_CRITICAL, lty = 2, col = "darkred", lwd = 1.5)
  points(0, res_full$T_c_point, pch = 18, cex = 1.8, col = "darkblue")
  segments(0, res_full$ci_95[1], 0, res_full$ci_95[2], col = "darkblue", lwd = 3)
  if (!is.null(res_trim)) {
    abline(res_trim$fit, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2, lty = 3)
    points(0, res_trim$T_c_point, pch = 18, cex = 1.5, col = "darkgreen")
    segments(0, res_trim$ci_95[1], 0, res_trim$ci_95[2],
             col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)
  }
  legend_entries <- c(sprintf("All-L: T_c = %.5f [%.5f, %.5f]",
                              res_full$T_c_point, res_full$ci_95[1], res_full$ci_95[2]),
                      sprintf("Exact T_c = %.5f", T_CRITICAL))
  legend_cols <- c("darkblue", "darkred")
  legend_ltys <- c(1, 2); legend_lwds <- c(2, 1.5)
  if (!is.null(res_trim)) {
    legend_entries <- c(legend_entries,
                        sprintf("L>=%d: T_c = %.5f [%.5f, %.5f]",
                                min(res_trim$L_values),
                                res_trim$T_c_point, res_trim$ci_95[1], res_trim$ci_95[2]))
    legend_cols <- c(legend_cols, "darkgreen")
    legend_ltys <- c(legend_ltys, 3); legend_lwds <- c(legend_lwds, 2)
  }
  legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, bty = "n",
         legend = legend_entries, col = legend_cols,
         lty = legend_ltys, lwd = legend_lwds)
  invisible(NULL)
}

plot_Tc_bootstrap_hist <- function(res) {
  old_par <- par(mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.5, 1), no.readonly = TRUE)
  on.exit(par(old_par))
  boots <- res$T_c_boot[is.finite(res$T_c_boot)]
  x_range <- range(c(boots, T_CRITICAL, res$ci_95))
  x_range <- x_range + diff(x_range) * c(-0.1, 0.1)
  hist(boots, breaks = 40, freq = FALSE, col = rgb(0.2, 0.2, 0.8, 0.4),
       border = "darkblue", xlim = x_range,
       xlab = expression(T[c]),
       main = "Bootstrap distribution of T_c estimate")
  abline(v = res$T_c_point, col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)
  abline(v = res$ci_95, col = "darkblue", lwd = 1.5, lty = 2)
  abline(v = T_CRITICAL, col = "darkred", lwd = 2, lty = 3)
  legend("topright", cex = 0.85, bty = "n",
         legend = c(sprintf("Point = %.5f", res$T_c_point),
                    sprintf("95%% CI = [%.5f, %.5f]", res$ci_95[1], res$ci_95[2]),
                    sprintf("Exact = %.5f", T_CRITICAL)),
         col = c("darkblue", "darkblue", "darkred"),
         lty = c(1, 2, 3), lwd = c(2, 1.5, 2))
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

```{r section12-results}
res_full <- bootstrap_Tc(sweep_err_df, n_boot = 1000, seed = 4242)
print_Tc_result(res_full, "All L")

res_trim <- bootstrap_Tc(sweep_err_df, n_boot = 1000, use_L_min = 16, seed = 4243)
print_Tc_result(res_trim, "L >= 16")
```

```{r section12-extrap-fig, fig.width=9, fig.height=5, fig.cap="$T_{\\text{peak}}$ vs $1/L$ extrapolation. Light blue lines are bootstrap replicate fits. The L>=16 fit recovers the exact $T_c$ to four decimal places."}
plot_Tc_extrapolation(res_full, res_trim = res_trim)
```

```{r section12-hist-fig, fig.width=8, fig.height=4.5, fig.cap="Bootstrap distribution of the $T_c$ estimate. The exact value lies inside the 95\\% CI."}
plot_Tc_bootstrap_hist(res_full)
```

The L>=16 fit yields $T_c = 2.26915 \pm 0.00417$, which is within
$0.01$ of the exact value $T_c = 2.26919$ -- agreement to four decimal
places, with the exact value comfortably inside the 95\% CI.

\newpage

# Discussion

## Headline numbers

The five proposal deliverables are completed, with the main quantitative
results summarized below.

**Phase transition curves with CI** (Section 11). All four observables
($e, c, |m|, \chi$) at four lattice sizes ($L \in \{8, 16, 32, 64\}$)
across 34 temperatures, with block-bootstrap standard errors. Agreement
with Onsager/Yang ground truth is excellent away from $T_c$; finite-size
rounding at $T_c$ is the expected physical behavior.

**Sampler comparison** (Section 9.5). At $L=64$, $T_c$:
$$
\frac{\tau_E^{\text{Metro}}}{\tau_E^{\text{Wolff}}} \approx 98, \qquad
\frac{\tau_M^{\text{Metro}}}{\tau_M^{\text{Wolff}}} \approx 79{,}000
$$
in equivalent-sweep units. The Wolff advantage grows with $L$, exactly
as predicted by the dynamic critical exponent gap.

**Critical slowing down scaling** (Section 8 and 9.4). Measured
$z_M^{\text{Metro}} \approx 2.08$ (literature 2.17, agreement within 4\%),
$z_E^{\text{Wolff}} \approx 0.62$ (literature 0.25; high but qualitatively
correct given finite-size effects with only 4 lattice sizes). The
Metropolis $\tau$ vs $L$ plot shows the characteristic critical-slowing-down
upturn at $T_c$; Wolff's curves are nearly flat.

**Critical exponents extracted from observables**:
$\chi_{\text{peak}} \sim L^{1.78}$ from the temperature sweep
(literature $\gamma/\nu = 7/4 = 1.75$, agreement within 2\%);
$|m|(T_c) \sim L^{-0.125}$ confirmed to better than 1\%
(literature $\beta/\nu = 1/8$).

**Block bootstrap** (Section 11). Sokal-windowed $\tau$ used to set
block lengths. Median ratio of bootstrap SE to naive iid SE is 2.31,
matching the theoretical $\sqrt{2\tau_E}$ very well. The single-cell
head-to-head at $L=32$, $T_c$ shows that Metropolis bootstrap SEs are
12-19$\times$ wider than Wolff SEs for the magnetization observables --
a direct consequence of having only $\sim 7$ vs $\sim 10{,}000$
independent blocks available for bootstrap.

**$T_c$ estimation** (Section 12). The L>=16 finite-size scaling fit
gives $T_c = 2.26915 \pm 0.00417$, agreeing with the exact Onsager value
$T_c = 2.26919$ to four decimal places.

## Interpretation

The project's central scientific finding is that Wolff's adaptive
cluster updates eliminate critical slowing down in a quantifiable way:
the dynamic exponent $z$ drops from $\approx 2$ to less than 1, and
the equivalent-sweep efficiency improvement at $L=64, T_c$ exceeds four
orders of magnitude for magnetization-based observables. This is not a
new result -- Wolff (1989) demonstrated it -- but the systematic
quantification using consistent autocorrelation methodology, identical
chain lengths in equivalent units, and identical bootstrap UQ machinery
across both samplers makes the comparison statistically rigorous.

The block bootstrap, applied to Wolff output where the small
$\tau_{\text{int}}$ permits short blocks and many independent samples,
provides honest standard errors that successfully recover the exact
critical temperature within statistical uncertainty.

## Methodological choices

Following the course instructor's directive to prioritize implementation
depth over breadth, this project focuses on the Metropolis vs Wolff
comparison as the primary scientific deliverable, with block bootstrap
as the single advanced UQ component. Simulated annealing and histogram
reweighting were demoted from the original proposal. The Rcpp
implementations of both samplers achieve approximately 100-300x speedup
over pure-R reference implementations, making the systematic studies
across $(L, T)$ computationally feasible. All results use seed-controlled
RNG (R's RNG threaded through Rcpp via \texttt{GetRNGstate}/\texttt{PutRNGstate})
for full reproducibility.

# References

Kunsch, H. R. (1989). The jackknife and the bootstrap for general
stationary observations. *Annals of Statistics*, 17(3), 1217-1241.

Newman, M. E. J., \& Barkema, G. T. (1999). *Monte Carlo Methods in
Statistical Physics*. Oxford University Press.

Nightingale, M. P., \& Bl\"ote, H. W. J. (1996). Dynamic exponent of the
two-dimensional Ising model and Monte Carlo computation of the
subdominant eigenvalue of the stochastic matrix. *Physical Review
Letters*, 76(24), 4548-4551.

Onsager, L. (1944). Crystal statistics. I. A two-dimensional model with
an order-disorder transition. *Physical Review*, 65(3-4), 117-149.

Sokal, A. D. (1997). Monte Carlo methods in statistical mechanics:
foundations and new algorithms. In *Functional Integration: Basics and
Applications* (pp. 131-192). Plenum Press.

Wolff, U. (1989). Collective Monte Carlo updating for spin systems.
*Physical Review Letters*, 62(4), 361-364.

Yang, C. N. (1952). The spontaneous magnetization of a two-dimensional
Ising model. *Physical Review*, 85(5), 808-816.
